actor1: Police Forces of Tajikistan (1994-) Prison Guards
notes: As reported on 27 March, inmates of a penal colony in Dushanbe complained of torture and extortion of money. Reportedly the prisoners are frequently beaten or bullied and have to pay to the officers to be treated better. The authorities deny any such accusation, but promised investigation.

As reported on 29 September, law enforcers of the Isfara district, Tajikistan, have been summoning or even detaining residents of the district - parents of young men as a form of pressure in order to make them to join the military forces of Tajikistan. The authorities of Isfara denied the statements and complains, claiming that the residents have been only invited for conversation or explanatory work. [coded according to the version of the civilians]
On 4 June 2018, in Bishkek, about 30 people protested demanding release of the leaders of El Unu opposition movement.

actor1: Police Forces of Tajikistan (1994-) Prison Guards
notes: In the week of the holiday Qurban (around 9 August), a detained woman in Rudaki village of Vakhsh in Khatlon region was raped by the local police officers.

In mid-September, police forces and military forces were deployed to Khorugh after a visit of the President, and stayed there for several months to combat 'lawlessness,' which raised the tensions with local residents of the region.

As reported on 8 March, in February 2019 around 10 people attacked a Muslim man in Tamchy for being a friend with a Christian man who had been beaten up over his faith in October 2018. Threats against Christians flared up after this incident.

As reported on 8 March 2019, in December 2018 a Protestant Christian man was beaten by several men in a local market in Tamchy who shouted 'we know you are a Christian wand we will punish you'. He left the village afterwards for fears for his safety. Two months earlier another Christian man had been severely beaten up.

assoc_actor_2: IRPT: Islamic Renaissance Party of Tajikistan
notes: In the summer of 2018, a man was arrested as part of a group of 15 former members of the banned IRPT. Shortly after his release, he committed suicide leaving a video where he declared having been tortured by law enforcements [coded to Khujand].

In the beginning of November 2018, a worker of the Junda plant was beaten by unidentified individuals in Kara-Balta. The plant which had been described for not paying the salaries of its workers. He was previously warned not to interfere in the case.

As reported on 20 April, in mid-March a resident of Muminabad was reportedly taken to a local police office and severely beaten by law enforcers, because of his state of intoxication by alcohol. According to his relative, even one month later he still suffered from the consequences of the beating. Exact date of the attack was not specified.

In July 2018, police detained a man in Yovon district [coded to Yovon] and beat, kicked and gave him electric shocks until he confessed of having committed a stabbing. The victim of the stabbing later stated that the suspect was not the perpetrator and he was released.

In early January 2019, an elderly female resident of Aini district, Sughd region was arrested and reportedly beaten by police officers in order to provide confession to an alleged crime. Later she had to be transferred to a hospital with serious injuries.

In the night of 2 to 3 August, a verbal altercation ended in a fight between two local residents of Solton-Sary and several Chinese gold miners. Two Kyrgyz men were injured and hospitalized. Tensions had been high between residents and employees of the Chinese goldmine after the mass death of livestock in the region, associated in the opinion of local residents with the mining activities.
Around 18 December 2018, about 50 workers building the Balykchy-Korumdu road gathered in Cholpon-Ata to protest against the non-payment of their salaries since the summer of the same year.

On 18 December 2019, about 600 protesters demonstrated in the Ala-Too main square in Bishkek in support of free speech and against corruption in the Kyrgyz state. Protest was sparked by Matrayimov family lawsuit against three news organizations that published investigative report on money laundering in Kyrgyzstan.

On 16 December 2019, about 20 villagers in Kyzyl-Unkur village of Bazar-Korgon district protested against reappointment of the director of the local forest park. Villagers insisted he is unfit for the job due to his unprofessional conduct toward the local population.

On 19 December 2019, about 55 supporters of the former lawmaker Sadyr Japarov who is inprison, gathered at the Gorky square in Bishkek city wearing t-shirts with his image and placard for his release from jail. Former MP was sentenced to 11 years and 6 months in prison for organizing mass protests.

On 23 December 2019, dozens of truck drivers gathered outside the Kyrgyz government representative office in Talas city demanding intervention in their problems with the road police in Kazakhstan due to excessive fines for traffic violations. Kyrgyz authorities responded by intiating talks with the Kazakh countrparts through the Ministry of Foreign Affairs.

On 26 December 2019, 10-15 relatives and supporters of arrested Kyrgyz politician protested outside the main government building in Bishkek city demanding fair justice and his release from detention. He was detained on 12 December and charged with provoking the mass riots in 7-8 August at the former Kyrgyz president's mansion.

On 27 December 2019, the coordinator of a contemporary feminist art-exhibition in Bishkek city was attacked by two rioters near her home in the late night hours. The exhibtion had received a lot of reaction from anti-feminism groups in late-November.

On 21 December 2019, 20-30 villagers in the town of Itagar gathered in the center of the village to protest against rising number of cattle theft in the area. Protesters expressed their dissatisfaction with the local authorities and police for being unable to curb the crime problem.

On 19 December 2019, 15-20 protesters demonstrated outside the Supreme Court in Bishkek city demanding dismissal of the head of the Worker's Union in Kyrgyzstan. Protesters accused the head of union of misuse of funds and mismanagement of properties.

On 31 December 2019, three brothers were detained and tortured by the Tajik Police in Dushanbe, Tajikistan. The counter account has been given by the Ministry of Internal Affairs that asserts one of the detained was arrested on the 30 December while selling illegal narcotics; the Ministry is silent on the account of two other brothers. Meanwhile the mother of the detained insists her son was tortured into confessing the crime.

On 16 December 2019, 30-40 Kyrgyz villagers from Kok-Tash town in Batken district protested against the fence that was being installed by the Tajik villagers. Kyrgyz Border Service officers who were called in to the scene ordered to halt any construction on the disputed territory which Tajik villagers refused to do so. No further information, no report of physical violence.

On 11 January 2020, rioters from Tajikistan threw stones at the vehicles belonging to Kyrgyz villagers in Tort-Kocho location near Kok-Tash village in Batken district that caused a verbal skirmish with the Kygryz villagers. Two groups then started to throw stoned to each other. Conflict was suppressed after intervention of the police and border services of the two countries .

On 15 January 2020, about 50 protesters gathered in Batken town of Batken region with the release of four villagers from the town of Kok-Tash near the border with Tajikistan. The villagers were detained after violent conflict broke out near Kok-Tash village between Tajik and Kyrgyz villagers on 11 January.

On 22 January 2020, about 150 supporters of the imprisoned former lawmaker Sadyr Japarov demonstrated outside the district administration's office in Tup village of Issyk Kul region demanding release of former MP from prison.

assoc_actor_2: Muslim Group (Tajikistan); Teachers (Tajikistan); Muslim Brotherhood (Tajikistan); Government of Tajikistan (1994-); Students (Tajikistan)
notes: As reported on 20 January 2020, over the previous two weeks a total of 70 people were detained on the suspicion of membership in the Muslim Brotherhood. Teachers, governmental employees, writers, students were reported to be among them. The exact locations of the arrests are unclear, but the media reports mention the arrests took place in different locations across the country, with the majority most likely in Dushanbe.

As reported on 28 January 2020, over the previous week, the arrests of more people that are suspected of membership in Muslim Brotherhood continued in Tajikistan. The number of arrested people has increased from 27 to at least 113. Teachers, governmental employees, writers, students were claimed to be among the detained. The exact locations are not clear but the media reports mention the arrests took place in different locations in the country, with the majority of the detained being in Dushanbe. The report specifically emphasizes the arrests of the relatives of the prominent religious leaders of Tajikistan, who have lately been under the close attention and control of the Tajik government, due to their important role in the Tajik society.

On 30 January 2020, a group of villagers from Kok-Serek village near Tengi town in Ala-Buka district of Kyrgyzstan gathered to protest the Uzbek border guards after they attempted to install a water canal on the claimed Kyrgyz territory. The border guards stopped their work upon the protest.
On 30 January 2020, about 100 residents of At-Bashi district gathered in the town of At-Bashi in protest of the planned China-funded logistics hub in the area.

On 28 January 2020, about 400 villagers protested outside the At-Bashi district administration's office in the town of At-Bashi against the planned Chinese funded logistical hub in the district. Protesters demanded the Kyrgyz authorities stop to China's involvement in the project.

On 6 February 2020, 200-500 residents of the Naryn region gathered in the village of Kalinin which is part of Ak-Tal town to protest against the $280 million Chinese investor funded logistics hub project in the area. Protesters believe the land where Chinese funded project is planned to be built, will be transferred to China in the future in exchange for debt.

On 6 February 2020, about 33 quarantined Kyrgyz truckers protested at the Torugart border post on the Kyrgyzstan-China border against lack of food in the facility. Truckers demanded border service provide food which truckers will pay for.

On 10 February 2020, about 200 local villagers of the town of Sogondu in Alai district of Osh region protested against the nearby Taigak-Tash coal mining project by gathering near the mine. Protesters expressed concern over an environmental impact of the coal mine on the local river and pice trees in the area.

On 17 February 2020, 300-400 local villagers protested against the Chinese investment logistics hub project in At-Bashi district of eastern Naryn region in Kyrgyzstan. Protesters said that there is an information about 200 hectares of land that was already given to China for the project and the local Kyrgyz villagers are categorically against it.

On 15 February 2020, public protest against the Chinese investor-funded logistics hub project took place in At-Bashi town of At-Bashy district in Naryn region of Kyrgyzstan. Protesters demanded the Kyrgyz government show them an official document which clarifies whether the land was leased to the Chinese company.

On 16 February 2020, about 30 residents protested in Ozgur village of Osh region demanding the local authorities resolve their land dispute with the private company. Protesters said this dispute has been in the courts since 2016 and has yet to come to a conclusion.

On 12 March 2020, 30-40 residents of Chym-Korgon village in Kemin district of Chui region protested outside the local administration's office against negligent transfer of 1 km border to the neighboring Kazakhstan. Protesters claimed the local construction businesses mine rubble and stone from the banks of nearby river that divides the two countries which resulted in riverbed changing its direction. The local administration officials told protesters the special commission was created to investigate the issue.

On 11 March 2020, a group of Kyrgyz villagers protested against the Uzbek villagers, who intruded into the Kyrgyz territory in Tash-Aryk village near the town of Tengi in Ala-Buka district of Jalal-Abad region, to dig a small aqueduct which would open access to water source on the Kyrgyz side. The Kyrgyz villagers demanded the Uzbek villagers stop digging to which the Uzbek villagers claimed it's their territory.
On 3 January 2020, 5-10 residents of Jibek-Jolu village in Kara-Suu town vicinity of Naryn region protested demanding access to drinking water.

On 31 March 2020, more than dozen of Mayak village residents protested against the local authorities initiated plan to open a coronavirus quarantine facility for possibly infected patients near their village in Tup district of Issyk-Kul region. Protesters expressed worries they may get infected if a quarantine facility goes into operation.

On 1 April 2020, a group of Mayak village residents protested against the local authorities' plan to open a quarantine facility for possibly infected with coronavirus patients near their village in Tup district of Issyk-Kul region. The local authorities were forced to abandon the plan after demonstrations.

On 1 April 2020, a group of unknown people broke the windows of the children's summer camp building in the village of Mayak in Tup district of Issyk-Kul region. It was a possibility that the attackers were part of the movement opposing the authorities' initial plan to open a quarantine facility at the summer camp for infected or possibly infected patients with coronavirus.

On 4 April 2020, a villager of Ak-Buura town in Kara-Suu district of Osh region was physically attacked by the residents of the nearby Uchar village after he refused to let Uchar villagers through a roadblock at the entrance of the town. The roadblock was voluntarily installed by the local community to keep out the possibly infected people with coronavirus. The victim was hospitalized with injures.

On 17 April 2020, the Council of Ul_m_ of the Islamic Center in Tajikistan has banned praying in the mosques for people of Tajikistan for the whole period of Ramadan month, in light of the 'coronavirus' outbreak in the rest of the world.

On 24 April 2020, 20-30 villagers and farmers protested at the nearby police quarantine roadblock as part of coronavirus measures in the village of Jany-Jer in Batken district, against the lack of irrigation water supply which protesters insisted is having a negative impact on their crop fields. Protesters demanded the local authorities resolve the issue.
On 7 May 2020, 15-20 residents of Telman village in Ysyk-Ata district protested against decrease in the milk prices, set by the wholesalers whom protesters sell it to.

On 9 May 2020, more than 50 residents of Acha-Kaindy village in At-Bashy district of Naryn region protested in the vicinity against coronavirus quarantine lockdown in their town as it reportedly impeded their seasonal farming work and activity near or outside the village. Protesters called for the Kyrgyz authorities to remove police roadblocks.

On 10 August 2018, an improvised explosive device was detonated on the intersection of Isanova and Ryskulova streets in Bishkek city targeting a Russian businessman at his office. No casualties were reported aside from minor injuries suffered by the Russian national. Kyrgyz media reported this was the second assassination attempt on the same Russian citizen's life after the first one in 2016.

On 28 April 2019, about 4 residents of the Tajik exclave Vorukh threw stones at the Kyrgyz villager's pickup truck damaging the vehicle's windshield near the village of Ak-Sai. Kyrgyz villager was driving through Vorukh because that is the only road Kyrgyz villagers use to get to the pastureland outside the Tajik village.

Residents of the district claimed that the detention took place unlawfully and the detainees were young and innocent. The officers shot into the air to disperse the crowd but protest continued.

On 25 May 2020, residents of the Rushan district of the Gorno-Badakhshan Autonomous Region started a protest near the local department of the State Committee for National Security in the district center, demanding the release of three people from the region after the the district authorities detained them over charges of drug trafficking. Residents of the district claimed that the detention took place unlawfully and the detainees were young and innocent. While no specific intervention was reported, all roads were blocked between the GBAO districts reportedly to prevent more residents of other areas coming to join protesters.

Residents of the district claimed that the detention took place unlawfully and the detainees were young and innocent. On 29 May 2020, residents of the Rushan district of the Gorno-Badakhshan Autonomous Region started a protest near the local department of the State Committee for National Security in the district center, demanding the release of three people from the region after the the district authorities detained them over charges of drug trafficking. Residents of the district claimed that the detention took place unlawfully and the detainees were young and innocent. While no specific intervention was reported, all roads were blocked between the GBAO districts reportedly to prevent more residents of other areas coming to join protesters.

On 18 May 2020, dozens of quarantined rioters, who previously arrived from Russia, attacked medics and attempted to vandalize an ambulance vehicle at a coronavirus observation facility close to the Manas airport near Dacha-Suu hamlet in Sokuluk district of Chui region. The incident took place after unconfirmed rumors spread in the camp that some infected patients have been dicharged and allowed to continue self-isolation at home which then sparked a riot.

On 22 May 2020, about 40 residents of Ak-Kya village in Ak-Talaa district of Naryn region protested against coronavirus quarantine roadblocks in the village demanding the local authorities remove them and let the farmers and villagers go back to work in the field due to a season in full swing. Protesters complained the medical teams shouldn't work at night to transport infected patients out of town and do so during day hours.

On 18 May 2020, 50-100 residents of Jeti-Oguz village in Issyk-Kul region protested against the local authorities plan to use the village clinic as an observation facility for patients that may have been infected with coronavirus. Protesters opposed the plan and demanded such facility operates elsewhere in the district but not in their town.

On 28 May 2020, 15-20 residents of Chon-Daly village in Ysyk-Ata district of Chui region protested against the local gravel pit mine which protesters complained was negatively impacting their village due to collateral dust blanketing their homes. Protesters demanded the local authorities resolve their public health and environmental concerns.

On 11 January 2019, 15-20 taxi drivers protested outside Tolubai cafe in Balykchy city against the new road safety and public conduct laws which carry steep fines for breaking traffic rules and public misconduct.

On 18 May 2020, about 10 villagers of the town of Telman in Chui region protested as they found the price of locally produced milk set by the wholesalers very low. Protesters demanded local authorities to get involved to resolve the issue.

On 4 January 2019, 20-30 residents of Kok-Moinok village protested against the nearby uranium mining project Tash-Bulak on the deposit premises in Issyk-Kul region demanding complete shut down of the mining activity due to environmental concerns.

On 1 February 2018, about 100 residents of Toguz-Toro district protested against the gold processing plant in Kazarman town demanding the Kyrgyz authorities shut down the project due to environmental concerns.

On 14 February 2019, 15-20 residents of the village of Burgondu in Kadamjai district of Batken region protested against several members of the Jehovas Witness Christian religious group who were in town to promote their group's beliefs among villagers.

On 12 May 2018, more than 50 residents of Ala-Buka town protested against the Chinese mining company operated Istamberdy gold project in the area expressing their concerns for environmental contamination.

On 15 July 2019, about 6 activists protested in the Maxim Gorky park in Bishkek city against the regional court's ruling to dismiss charges against suspects in rape case from 2013. Protesters demanded the Kyrgyz authorities revise the court ruling and bring the rape perpetrators to justice.

assoc_actor_1: SDPK: Social Democratic Party of Kyrgyzstan
notes: On 16 July 2019, dozens of supporters of former Prime-Minister Sapar Isakov gathered in the village of Kochkor of Naryn region in protest of his detention and to announce a creation of the campaign in defense of detained former PM who was under criminal investigation in corruption case.

On 7 August 2019, about 200 residents of Naryn region gathered close to the village of Kara-Chiy near Solton-Sary gold project demanding all mining activity by the Chinese state affiliated company halted and mining equipment removed from the mine.

On 13 July 2019, about 20 residents of Tup district in Issyk-Kul region gathered at the Karkyra summer pasture calling for the country's president to revise imprisoned Kyrgyz politician Sadyr Japarov's case demanding his release from prison.

On 7 February 2018, 500-1000 residents of Toguz-Toro district protested in the town of Kazarman against the gold processing plant project in the area due to environmental concerns demanding the Kyrgyz authorities shut down the plant.
On 27 February 2018, about 50 villagers in Kadamjai district protested near the village of Maidan against the Shambesai gold mining project due to environmental concerns.

On 10 March 2018, 200-300 residents of Toguz-Toro district protested in the town of Kazarman against the gold processing plant project in the area demanding the Kyrgyz authorities shut it down due to environmental concerns.

On 2 February 2018, about 1000 residents of Kadamjai district protested near Maidan village against the Shambesai gold mining project expressing their concerns for pollution of the local river which is vital water source for surrounding villages in the area.

On 19 October 2019, a group of Kyrgyz villagers protested on the main road in Zhaka-Oruk neighborhood near Ak-Sai village in Batken region demanding Tajik counterparts remove road bumps they have installed previously. Protesters called for the Kyrgyz officials to get involved in conflict resolution between Kyrgyz and Tajik villagers.

On 24 April 2019, 5-10 environmental activists protested at the Tash-Bulak uranium deposit near Kok-Moinok village demanding shut down of the project. Protesters stopped a cargo truck which was coming out of the mine during a protest and ordered a driver to open the trailer and let protesters check on the cargo items. No violent interaction.

On 21 January 2019, about 20 residents of Safarovka village in Jalal-Abad region protested near the local school against closure and transfer of the town's public school to the private business. The protesters also demanded the Suzak district's head of administration resign because of his decision regarding transfer.

On 8 October 2018, several protesters demonstrated outside the White House in Bishkek city demanding the Kyrgyz president intervene in their court case to speed up the hearing process which protesters argued was too slow.

assoc_actor_1: SDPK: Social Democratic Party of Kyrgyzstan
notes: On 24 October 2018, 50-60 supporters of former presidential adviser Ikramjan Ilmiyanov who was a member of the ruling SDPK political faction protested in the village of Katran in Leilek district of Batken region against his detention demanding fair investigation of his case and change his pretrial detention to house arrest.

assoc_actor_1: SDPK: Social Democratic Party of Kyrgyzstan
notes: On 7 December 2018, several supporters of the former Kyrgyz president protested at the Social Democratic Party's press-conference in Bishkek city against the party's new leadership demanding former president retains his chairmanship of the party. Security was called in to escort the protesters out of the building.

On 4 December 2018, about 100 residents of Terek-Sai village protested against the detention of their 15 fellow villagers who were probed by police for assaulting several policemen on 5 November 2018 at the nearby gold mining project over trespassing incident.

On 6 December 2018, about 100 protesters demonstrated in the town of Batken against dismissal of their representative in the central government's regional office demanding his job back and reinstatement in the position of deputy.

On 4 December 2018, about 10 clients of the Aman bank demonstrated outside the bank's office in the town of Kurshab of Uzgen district demanding their deposits back after bank failed to return it. Protesters called for the Kyrgyz authorities to revoke the bank's license until they will receive their money back.
On 26 June 2019, dozens of protesters demonstrated outside the Prosecutor General's Office in Bishkek city demanding justice for a victim of property scam which left her homeless.

On 25 December 2018, about 30 residents of the village of Boz-Adyr protested at the local administration demanding answers regarding rumors of possible transfer of their nearby land and water source to the neighboring Uzbekistan.

On 27 December 2018, about 300 residents of Min-Bulak village in Naryn region protested in the their town against the Chinese mining company managed gold project Solton Sary demanding halt to mining activity in the area due to environmental concerns.

On 11 April 2019, about 100 small shop owners of the Bereket market at the Osh bazaar in Bishkek city protested outside their shops against closure of the market which protesters claimed wasn't known to them until the last minute. Protesters insisted it was illegal to shut down the market without at least a month prior advance notice.

On 4 September 2019, several residents of the Issyk-Kul region protested near Balykshy town against the Tash-Bulak uranium mining project demanding the interagency commission members that have conducted an environmental research into the project's impact remain impartial.

On 19 April 2019, about 50 shop owners of the Bereket market at the Osh bazaar in Bishkek city rioted against the market management's move to close down the market and restrict access to the shop owners resulting in rioters storming through the locked rear entrance gate and kicking several security guards out of the market. Rioting shop owners demanded full access to their shops.

On 23 January 2019, dozens of employees of the state-owned circus protested outside the Ministry of Culture in Bishkek city against the appointment of the new director demanding Kyrgyz officials support their own candidate for the directorship of the circus.

On 3 September 2019, hundreds of retail shop owners protested at the Bereket company owned pavilion in Osh bazaat in Bishkek city demanding the city administration intervenes in their dispute with the company and retains its ownership of the pavilion to keep it under control of the city authorities. Protesters demanded the city provides electricity to their businesses after Bereket cut power supply over dispute.

actor1: Military Forces of Uzbekistan (2016-) Border Service
notes: On 7 April 2018, a Kyrgyz citizen was shot and killed by the Uzbek border guard on the undemarcated border in the Kyrgyz neighborhood of Mamay near the town of Kerben in Aksy district of Jalal-Abad region. Deadly incident took place when victim crossed the border from Uzbekistan into Kyrgyzstan after visiting his relatives on the Uzbek side. Uzbek Border service defended its border guard's action by indicating that Kyrgyz citizen ignored warnings prior to his death.

On 3 September 2019, about 20 Tajik villagers protested in the bordering village of Orto-Boz near Ak-Tatyr demanding the Kyrgyz villager remove his car from the disputed land where he set up a shop to sell goods out of his car. Kyrgyz villagers responded by insisting that the land was leased for 49 years by the Kyrgyz side and it was legal to do business on the disputed territory.

On 21 June 2018, about 30 construction workers protested at the Maxim Gorky park in Bishkek city against the KG Group company demanding their back pay for months of work which protesters insisted the company owes them.

On 11 October 2019, more than several relatives of the deceased victim rioted at the Chui regional court in Bishkek city throwing bottles at a judge demanding he speaks to them to explain the reasons for acquittal of suspects in the homicide case resulting in death of their family member. Policemen were forced to intervene to protect a judge from rioters outside the court as judge was leaving the scene in his personal automobile.

On 27 September 2019, more than several villagers of Chong-Alai district rioted at a mining project managed by a Chinese firm, near the village of Daroot-Korgon. They demanded an explanation from the Chinese nationals regarding an earlier incident where a Chinese miner allegedly threatened a Kyrgyz man with a knife. Rioters blocked the Chinese workers' vehicle at the project demanding them to come out.

On 15 March 2019, about 50 protesters demonstrated near the village of Ak-Sai in Batken region at the meeting with the Kyrgyz parliament and government officials demanding the central authorities resolve the border dispute with Tajikistan.

On 3 June 2020, about 30 residents of Chaek village in Jumgal district of Naryn region protested near their homes demanding the local authorities shutdown nearby landfill, provide aid to the households that have been affected by flashflood day before, fix drinking water supply and clean up irrigation canals in the village.

On 2 June 2020, a Kyrgyz national was rushed to the hospital after sustaining a gunshot wound while driving on Osh-Isfana motor road in Eki-Tash neighborhood near Samarkandyk village of Batken district in Batken region. Multiple hunting rifle fire shots were discharged from the territory of Tajikistan by unknown individuals who were shooting at the Kyrgyz citizen's automobile.

On 5 June 2020, about 170 residents and farmers of Jerge-Tal village in Naryn district of Naryn region blocked the local road to Kum-Bel pastures in protest of unresolved dispute regarding limited access to grazing land for out of town cattle farmers.

On 31 May 2020, a riot broke out between dozens of Kyrgyz and Uzbek nationals after dispute regarding access to water source near the village of Chechme in Kadamjai district of Batken region on the border with Uzbekistan near the village of Chashma in Sokh enclave. Rioters hurled rocks at each other while setting few properties on fire on both sides of the border. 200 Uzbek citizens were hospitalized in Sokh enclave including 13 with gunshot wounds and 25 Kyrgyz villagers were taken to the hospital in Kadamjai district with injuries of various degrees.

On 1 June 2020, dozens of people have gathered in front of the administration of Rushan district of the Gorno-Badakhshan Autonomous Region to deliver their concerns regarding the arrest of the four local citizens, who have escaped and are currently missing and searched by the Tajik authorities. People consider these arrests unlawful.

On 27 May 2020, a Kyrgyz citizen opened fire from Koktash village of Kyrgyzstan and wounded a Tajik woman in Chorku, a Tajik village along the Tajik- Kyrgyz border. According to the border official from both countries, the shooting may have taken place as a provocation as the border violence is going on due to the use of pasture in the undemarcated areas between Kyrgyzstan and Tajikistan.

On 17 May 2020, dozens of residents from Khuroson, Khatlon region protested by blocking the road from Bokhtar to the capital. The crowd critized government's inactivity to recontrust people's houses damaged by torrent earlier in May, demanding immediate involvement.

Property destruction: On 7 June 2020, an unknown group of arsonists threw Molotov cocktails into the local office of the independent Channel-3 broadcasting station in the town of Talas, very early in the morning. TV station's journalists insisted was directly connected to their work. Director of Channel-3 indicated that about $15,000 worth of equipment was destroyed in fire.

On 17 June 2020, about 50 residents of Kadamjai district protested outside the town administration's office in Kyzyl-Kia township against the nearby coal mine auction which left them jobless. Protesters complained they have loans to pay and the coal mine was providing jobs until it was sold to the private businesses.

On 14 June 2020, about 50 residents and livestock farmers protested at the Kara-Kujur valley near the village of Jerge-Tal in Naryn district of Naryn region against the local administration's plan to extend shared use of Kara-Kujur pastures for cattle farmers. Protesters categorically opposed an extension arguing there is a shortage of grazing land in the area.

On 19 June 2020, about 20 protesters demonstrated outside the city court in Talas city against a judge whom protesters accused of corruption after main suspect in fatal car accident case was acquitted of criminal charges. Protesters argued a judge was bribed in exchange for favorable verdict.

On 16 June 2020, about 300 residents of Ken-Bulun village in Chui region protested against the local police and court failure to investigate an assault incident which took place 2 days earlier when nearby restaurant security guards attacked fellow villagers.

On 16 June 2020, people gathered in front of the local administration in Rushan town of Gorno-Badakhshan Autonomous region, demanding authorities to solve the case relating to deteetion of three people from the region, over charges of drug trafficking. The detentions took place on May 22 and sparked several demonstrations earlier.

On 3 July 2020, about 10 residents of Esenjan Saparbekov street in the village of Chaek of Jumgal district in Naryn region protested against bad roads in the neighborhood demanding the local administration fix the roads and restrict traffic for heavy duty trucks in the area.

Around 29 June 2020, several members of a Kyrgyz family protested in Kok-Tash village, Batken, against the Tajik border guards authorizing the demolition of their concrete fence by the Tajik villagers on the disputed Kyrgyz-Tajik borde.

On 31 May 2019, 15-20 garage owners from Dzhal neighborhood protested outside the Mayor's Office in Bishkek city against takeover of their properties by the private company which restricted access to protesters. Garage owners demanded the city administration intervene in their dispute and protect their ownership rights.

On 12 May 2020, two unidentified people wearing face-masks attacked and severely beaten a famous Tajik journalist in Dushanbe. According to the journalist the attack was preceded by numerous threats on a phone, related to journalist's criticism of the Tajik government in preventing coronavirus spread.

On 24 July 2020, 50-60 residents of Talas district protested outside the Jerooy gold mine camp in Talas region demanding the company shut down its operations for coronavirus quarantine after one of its workers died of pneumonia which protesters feared may spread in the district.

On 19 July 2020, several citizens of Pakistan were attacked and assaulted by 5-6 rioters at the mountain resort near the village of Koi-Tash in Alamudun district of Chui region after verbal altercation between groups which resulted in Pakistani nationals seeking medical aid in Bishkek city.

On 23 July 2020, about 20 local activists protested on the North-South motroway in the village of Kuyruchuk in Jumgal district of Naryn region against poor quality road construction work on the section of motorway, demanding the authorities to probe the construction firm for doing an improper job.

On 29 July 2020, about 65 protesters including relatives of a murder victim demonstrated outside the Mayor's Office in the town of Uzgen in Osh region demanding justice served in local cab driver murder case.

On 28 July 2020, about 6 entrepreneurs gathered in At-Bashi township of Naryn region in protest of spreading rumors that their investment project is funded by Chinese investor. Protesters called for the local anti-China activist Timur Makhmudov to stop injecting false information regarding their meat and milk plant in the district.

On 29 July 2020, 15-20 employees of the Issyk-Kul regional Forestry Department protested outside headquarters in Ananyevo village in Issyk-Kul region against head of the department demanding his dismissal over allegations of corruption.

On 12 August 2020, dozens of protesters, including citizens of Russia, demonstrated outside the Russian embassy in Dushanbe city demanding the Russian government organize a charter flight to evacuate its citizens and migrant workers from Tajikistan to Russia. Protesters complained they are stuck in Tajikistan due to coronavirus lockdown in both countries.

On 6 August 2020, a Kyrgyz villager was shot and wounded in Zhaka-Oruk locality near the village of Kok-Tash in Batken district of Batken region on the disputed border between Tajikistan and Kyrgyzstan. Attackers remained unknown.

On 6 August 2020, a resident of the village of Samonien in Isfara region of Tajikistan was shot dead a kilometer from the disputed Tajik-Kyrgyz border in the region. Attackers unknown. The incident came on the same day where a Kyrgyz villager and border guard were injured by unidentified attackers, possibly from Tajik side.

Around 31 July 2020, a 22-year-old conscript serviceman was pronounced dead after sustaining gunshot wound at the military base 87740 near Bokhtar township in Khatlon region of Tajikistan. The Military first claimed that the soldier shot himself by mistake but then arrested 4 servicemen from the same facility in connection with deadly shooting incident.

On 17 August 2020, about 50 residents of Kok-Tash village protested outside the district court in Batken town demanding release of four of their fellow villagers who were detained in January for alleged involvement in border-related clashes.

On 10 September 2020, 20-30 residents of Chatkal district protested outside the local administration in Kanysh-Kya township of Jalal-Abad region against the operating of gold mining companies in the area demanding the authorities tighten oversight after complaints about environmental pollution.

On 29 April 2019, hundreds of protesters gathered in the centre of Balykchy, protesting against the development of the Tash-Bulak uranium deposit in Issyk-Kul and for public hearings on this matter. The event was a part of the wider string of demonstrations against the deposit.

On 24 April 2019, about 50 protesters gathered outside the White House in Bishkek city, predominantly parents of Novopavlovka village high school students in Sokuluk district, demanding information about financial affairs of the school due to suspicions that funds were misused. Protesters called for the resignation of the high school director and shut down of the school's donation fund.

On 22 April 2019, about 300 people gathered at the Tash-Bulak uranium deposit near Kok-Moinok village in Ton district of Issyk-Kul region, demanding the mining of uranium to be stopped due to its potential evironmental effects.

On 25 June 2019, about 30 protesters demonstrated at a peaceful rally in support of labour migrants which was organized in Bishkek by the Zamandash party. Protesters called for better working conditions and insurance for workers.

On 24 November 2019, about 100 protesters rallied at the Bereket pavilion of Osh bazaar in Bishkek city which has had fire incidents on two occasions previously. The district authorities reported that the gathering occurred due to the distribution of spots in the newly rebuilt market.

On 15 January 2018, around 300 protesters gathered in the village Oruktu, Ala-Buka district demanding the Chinese-owned gold mining company settles outstanding social package taxes with the local township after which the mining operation can be resumed.

On 17 September 2020, about 10 rioters attacked and assaulted residents of Dyikan-Kyshtak village near the town of Kara-Suu in Osh region over a land property dispute during which rioters used a bulldozer to demolish private home construction projects.

On 13 May 2019, about 20 parents of the Novopavlovka high school students protested at the Maxim Gorky park in Bishkek city demanding resignation of the school principal and shut down of the school's donation fund which protesters insisted wasn't transparent.

On 14 May 2018, about 5 protesters demonstrated outside the General Prosecutor's Office in Bishkek city demanding chief prosecutors investigate an illegal eviction of the family from their home. Protesters complained the district court and local law enforcement are ignoring their case.

On 29 April 2019, dozens of parents of the high school students in Novopavlovka village in Sokuluk district protested on the premises against negative public campaign toward the school's leadership and parents initiated donation fund. Protesters insisted the school's fund is transparent and their kids are receiving the best support because of it.

On 18 September 2020, several residents of the town of Kant in Chui region protested at the local park on Chekirov street against the planned construction of the residential apartment building project on location demanding the town administration preserve the park for the neighbourhood.

On 12 March 2019, about 30 retail shop owners protested at the Dordoi clothing market in Bishkek city against proposed high tariffs on imported goods from China and other foreign states demanding the Kyrgyz government keeps tariffs low or else they will go out of business because of it.

On 6 September 2018, more than 50 kidney dialysis patients protested outside the Ministry of Health in Bishkek city demanding the health authorities provide them with safe medication for treatment rather than cheap and unsafe drugs which people with kidney conditions insisted have side effects.

On 7 June 2019, about 40 residents of Dachnoe village in Ysyk-Ata district of Chui region protested outside the local recycling facility demanding shut down of the operation due to negative environmental impact on the village.

On 2 April 2018, about 100 tanker truck drivers protested near the toll plaza in Sosnovka village of Chui region against police fines for absence of insurance policy which protesters insisted was aiding police corruption. Protesters urged the Kyrgyz government to revise the regulation and include the drivers' adjustments to the law requiring mandatory insurance.

On 26 March 2018, 10-15 teachers of the Kyrgyz State National University protested near the dormitory 9 in Bishkek city refusing to vacate the university owned building and demanding electricity turned back on which was cut due to planned renovation. Protesters complained they have nowhere else to go with their families.

On 14 May 2019, about 10 garage owners protested against demolition of their garages on Tugelbai Ata street in Bishkek city by the Mayor's Office to build a road. Protesters insisted demolition was illegal due to pending lawsuit to cancel the city administration's plan in the city court.

On 2 April 2018, 50-100 private bus drivers protested at the bus stop in 12th district of Bishkek city against police fines for absence of insurance policy. Protesters demanded the city administration suspend fines for private bus operators and increase bus fare within city limits.

On 14 February 2018, about 50 supporters of 3 imprisoned Kyrgyz politicians protested outside the White House in Bishkek city demanding resignation of a chief of the country's security service accusing him of abuse of power while in office.

On 22 April 2019, about 8 construction workers protested at the construction site near the International University of Science and Business on 7-April street in Bishkek city demanding their back pay from the private contractor whom protesters accused of improper conduct after they finished a job.

On 27 February 2019, 15-20 residents of the Turk-Abad village in Suzak district of Jalal-Abad region protested in town against the local councilman whom they voted for accusing him of misleading them before election and falsely promising land plots.

On 13 December 2019, 30-40 relatives and friends of homicide victim protested outside the city court in Tokmok city demanding justice in the case after judge ruled to release suspect from detention. Protesters accused a judge of corruption and called for the Kyrgyz authorities to intervene in the case.

On 16 January 2019, about 20 residents of the Dacha-Suu village in Sokuluk district protested at the memorial monument to the victims of air crash in 2017 demanding the Kyrgyz government deliver on its promises to fix the village infrastructure and roads.

On 18 June 2018, more than 20 residents of the Archa-Beshik neighborhood in Bishkek city protested against bad roads in the area demanding the city authorities fix the local infrastructure and stop ignoring their issues.

On 24 December 2019, about 30 cargo truck drivers protested at the Ak-Tilek Avtodorozhny checkpoint near Hun Chi village in Ysyk-Ata district of Chui region against discrimination of Kyrgyz truck drivers by the Kazakh border authorities and imposing administrative barriers by the Kazakh Customs for transiting Kyrgyz cargo trucks through Kazakhstan.

On 26 January 2018, about 20 female protesters demonstrated at the district court in the town of Toktogul expressing their frustration with a judge who dismissed their appeal in their lawsuit against local market owners whom the protesters accused of forging records and documents to take away their retail shops.

On 31 January 2018, about 13 female protesters demonstrated outside the White House in Bishkek city appealing to the country's president and the Supreme Court for intervention in their court case regarding a dispute with the market owners in the town of Toktogul.

On 8 May 2018, about 10 female protesters demonstrated outside the White House in Bishkek city against detention and prosecution of their family members that are suspected of attacking and destroying the Chinese company's gold plant in Toguz Toro district months earlier.
On 13 May 2019, parents gathered in village Novopavlovka in Sokuluk region to protest against gathering money from students by a local school. The parents demanded dismissal of the school director.

On 29 November 2019, residents of a newly constructed neighbourhood at the outskirts of Bishkek organized a protest after they received a notice that a minibus line which was taking them to the city would be abolished.

On 28 September 2018, a group of experts came to village Maidan in Kadamjai. Reportedly, they were related to a gold mining company. Their presence angered the locals who oppose gold mining activities in the village. The expers were forced into a container and locked up by the residents. Police arrived at the scene to clear up the conflict.

On 17 December 2018, a group of residents of Bishkek organized a protest after the government started demolishing their houses. The government representatives claimed that the houses had been constructed illegally. The protesters demanded a meeting with the authorities.

On 14 December 2018, about 15 victims of shopping centre fire at the Osh Bazaar in Bishkek city protested near the market demanding the city authorities allow them to operate their makeshift shops close to bazaar after suffering financial losses due to fire accident.

On 16 April 2018, about 20 private garage owners protested against the demolition of their properties in the Vostok-5 micro-district of Bishkek city demanding the local authorities remove construction equipment and stop all activity near the garage park.

On 13 June 2018, around 300 protesters gathered in Ala-Buka region against the operations of a gold-mining company. They demanded compensations and asked the authorities to investigate the ecological impact of the mining operations. The authorities met with the protesters.
On 22 November 2019, a group of Hepatitis B patients organized a protest in Bishkek and demanded from the government to introduce national vaccination for the disease.

On 7 March 2018, 10-15 construction workers protested outside the State National Hospital on the corner of Moskovsky and Panfilov streets in Bishkek city demanding their wages from the contractor firm and hospital.

On 19 June 2019, more than 100 landowners in village Voenno-Antonovka in Sokuluk region organized a protest. They protested as they have no right to construct houses on the lands they own because of the lands' agricultural status.

On 6 August 2018, activists organized a protest in Bishkek against the registration of a single candidate for the mayorship of Bishkek. The protesters claimed that the candidate is not suitable for the position.

On 13 June 2018, several residents and farmers of the Kaytpas village in Kadamjai district of Batken region protested against natural gas pipe removal works on their crop fields demanding a halt to all construction work due to damages inflicted upon their agricultural land.

On 24 October 2018, there was a brawl between the residents of Batken and representatives of a gold mining company who came to the city to take samples. The locals did not allow experts to work. Police interfered to calm down the citizens.

On 9 October 2018, a brief scuffle broke out outside the district court in Kara-Suu township of Osh region between dozens of people who came out to support Papan township Mayor and a group of opponents who accused a Mayor of corruption and abuse of power over an alleged bribery scheme involving land distribution deal. Police were called in to maintain public order at the scene of the altercation.

On 29 May 2019, trailer drivers in At-Bashi region organized a protest against raising tax fees. They claimed that the clients have found other routes to transport goods due to increased taxes. Drivers from Issyk-Kul region joined the protest.

On 2 May 2019, residents of village Internatsionalnoye in Ysyk-Ata district organized a protest against movement of large vehicles of a mining company. The villagers claimed that the vehicles damage their central road.
On 19 April 2019, residents of village Kok-Moinok in Ton district organized a protest against planned mining of uranium in the area. They claimed that mining would be dangerous for their health.
On 31 May 2019, residents of a dormitory in Bishkek organized a protest against the plans to evict them.

On 3 April 2019, 20-30 parents protested outside the local high school in Novopavlovka village in Sokuluk district of Chui region against mandatory monthly cash contribution to the school board demanding principal disclose financial records.

On 3 April 2018, 20-30 passenger mini-bus drivers protested at the bus stop in the 11th micro-district in Bishkek city against the discrimination and excessive traffic violation fines demanding the city authorities cooperate with protesters and remove costly fines.

On 4 June 2019, residents of multistore building organized a protest in Bishkek against the contruction of a building that is allegedly too close their house, demanding the government demolishing the building.

On 26 April 2018, 15-20 residents of Japalak village near Osh city protested against nearby landfill demanding the city authorities relocate it elsewhere due to public health and environmental concerns in the area.

On 17 September 2020, about 100 protesters demonstrated outside the regional court in Jalal-Abad city demanding the authorities drop all charges against their fellow villager from Suzak district and release him from pretrial detention.

On 18 September 2020, several male residents of Communism village near Kulundu township in Leilek district of Batken region protested against planting trees by the Tajik villagers on the disputed border calling for the Kyrgyz authorities to tighten monitoring on the Tajik-Kyrgyz border. Protesters proceeded to the perimeter where they manually removed a number of trees.
On 17 September 2020, 30-40 victims of a pyramid scheme protested at the financial firm's shop in Balykchy city demanding their funds back after news of its fraudulent activity.

assoc_actor_2: SDPT: Social Democratic Party of Tajikistan
notes: On 22 September 2020, two unknown people attacked the leader of the opposition Social Democratic Party of Tajikistan (SDPT) near his home in Dushanbe. According to the SDPT leader, he passed out for four-five minutes, and after he regained consciousness, the attackers were gone.

On 3 January 2020, dozens of residents of Khorugh (Shugnan) gathered in Khorugh demanding the release of the brother of one of the informal leaders of the region after the authorities detained him over the charges of disobeying police officers.

On 24 September 2020, 10-15 victims of Ponzi scheme gathered outside the police station in Balykchy city demanding the authorities assist them with recovering their funds back from the fraudulent company.

On 20 September 2020, a mass brawl broke out between about 100 rioters and supporters of the Mekenim Kyrgyzstan and Birimdik parties in Aravan township (Aravan district, Osh region) during a meeting of Mekenim Kyrgyzstan activists with voters when pro-government Birimdik party supporters allegedly disrupted campaigning process which then triggered the physical confrontation. 12 people were injured.

On 22 September 2020, Respublica party activist was attacked and assaulted in Alysh village (Naryn district, Naryn region) by rioters representing Mekenim Kyrgyzstan party after an argument regarding alleged violation of election law. Mekenim Kyrgyzstan denied the accusations.

On 29 September 2020, 5-10 owners of the catering and restaurant businesses protested outside the Mayor's office in Talas city demanding the authorities lift coronavirus quarantine restrictions on their businesses.

On 8 October 2020, about 400 workers of state bodies and others organized a rally in Kara-Suu town of Osh region, calling for stability following the riots in the capital Bishkek. The protestors also expressed their support to the incumbent Kyrgyz president Sooronbay Jeenbekov.

Around 7 October 2020, about 20 local villagers protested at the Bozymchak gold mine in Ala-Buka district of Jalal-Abad region demanding shut down of the mining operation as part of the series of nationwide opposition riots.

On 6 October 2020, about 500 protesters gathered on the main square in Naryn city, demanding the regional administration officials' resignation following anti-election protest and clashes in Bishkek city.

On 6 October 2020, about 100 protesters gathered outside the Mayor's Office in the town of Kyzyl-Kia in Batken region demanding the resignation of the town officials following the anti-government protest in Bishkek city.

On 6 October 2020, about 200 protesters gathered outside the regional administration's office in Talas city, demanding the regional top officials' resignation after following the anti-election demonstration and riot in Bishkek.

On 6 October 2020, 300-400 rioters intruded into the Ishtamberdy gold mining project operated by the Chinese company in Oruktu village of Ala-Buka district in Jalal-Abad region and forced the Chinese workers out of the mine as part of the series of nationwide opposition riots.

On 6 October 2020, a group of rioters occupied the gold mining project near the village of Chakmak-Suu in Chatkal district of Jalal-Abad region and attacked mine workers as part of the nation-wide opposition riots. The workers locked themselves in the mine laboratory calling for help and safety from the Interim government. No further information.

On 8 October 2020, 40-50 residents of the Sary-Zhon village in Ysyk-Ata district of Chui region gathered near contested land demanding the local administration provide land for residential homes or else protesters will seize it.

On 8 October 2020, about 100 truck drivers protested at the Abu-Sahiy logistics company's warehouse in the village of Kashgar-Kyshtak in Kara-Suu district of Osh region demanding the Kyrgyz government expropriate and nationalize the company over its alleged corruption record and monopoly.

On 6 October 2020, more than a dozen villagers protested at the Turkish company-operated gold mine in Buzuk locality near Terek-Sai in Chatkal district of Jalal-Abad region, demanding shut down of the mining operation until further notice.

On 6 October 2020, dozens of protesters gathered at the Mayor's Office in Jalal-Abad city demanding city officials resign after mass demonstration and riot against the parliamentary election results in Bishkek city.

On 7 October 2020, about 500 supporters and opponents of the local administration officials rioted outside the town hall in the town of Kerben in Aksy district of Jalal-Abad region as part of the series of nationwide opposition riots. During the demonstration physical confrontation broke out resulting in the hospitalization of six people with various injuries.

On 23 October 2020, 20-30 protesters demonstrated outside the district administration's office in the town of Uzgen, demanding the central authorities reappoint the former chief of the district administration back in his position.

On 22 October 2020, reportedly about 30 employees of Manas International airport protested in Dacha-Suu hamlet near Bishkek city demanding the newly appointed director resign and the Kyrgyz government hire young professionals to manage the country's main airport network.

On 10 January 2020, around midnight, in the area of Zhaka-Oruk near Kok-Tash village, Batken district, unidentified people threw stones at a Kyrgyz car heading to Batken. These persons were then reported to have hid in Tajik side of the border. Kyrgyz authorities reported that there are injured citizens of Kyrgyzstan from among those who were in the car. The car has broken rear and side windows. Also, later in the same night, it was claimed that some Tajik citizens had stoned the house of a Kyrgyz citizen in the Dakhma area near same village. After these incident, Kyrgyz border guards later opened fire in the air near the border. No report of a confrontation or injuries from that fire.

On 29 October 2020, about 50 protesters gathered outside the Talas district court in Talas city demanding justice in a fatal driving-while-intoxicated road collision case involving a former local official.

On 30 October 2020, about 20 protesters gathered outside the Barpy dramatic theater in Jalal-Abad city in protest of the prophet Muhammad caricature in France and French president Emmanuel Macron's statement which protesters disagreed with.

On 2 November 2020, teachers and daycare workers protested outside the district administration in the town of Cholpon-Ata in Issyk-Kul district of Issyk-Kul region against the reappointment of a former district education department official whom protesters accused of corruption, demanding the authorities remove the official from office.

On 4 November 2020, about 100 local villagers protested outside Bozymchak gold mining project in Ala-Buka district of Jalal-Abad region, demanding the project manager's removal due to alleged discrimination of district residents seeking jobs at the mine.

On 12 November 2020, coal delivery truck drivers protested in Balykchy city against excessive fees for overweight coal shipments and corruption in the coal supply chain, demanding the Kyrgyz government nullify its regulation from 2011, which protesters complained is breeding corruption.

On 16 November 2020, residents of Nookat district in Osh region protested in the town of Nookat against the newly-appointed head of the district administration demanding the authorities remove him from office.

On 18 November 2020, residents of Balykchy city protested outside the municipal water department demanding its director resign after water fee increase and accusing the official of job position abuse.

On 16 November 2020, dozens of truck drivers protested outside the Talas regional state administration in Talas city against long delays for costly coronavirus test results, which protesters complained takes extra several days on the border with Kazakhstan. Drivers demanded the Kyrgyz authorities install a virus test facility on the border and provide testing free of charge for truck drivers.

On 5 December 2019, about 15 protesters gathered outside the Pervomaisky district court in Bishkek in support of the online activist who manages a political news page on Facebook. He was detained on 24 November and accused of incitement of inter-regional tensions. Similarly, 20-30 supporters and relatives of former Kyrgyz officials gathered outside the Sverdlovsky district court in Bishkek demanding a fair trial for a number of high-ranking officials including 2 former prime-ministers. The officials were arrested and charged with corruption in the China-funded power-heating plant modernization project.

source: BBC News; Gezitter; AKIpress; Sanjar Kalmatai
notes: On 11 March 2019, 40 women organized a protest against the movement 'Feminist Initiative' in Bishkek. They disagreed with the opinion that 'the rights of girls are not protected in Kyrgyzstan. Likewise, about 8 members of Kyrk Choro movement protested outside the city administration in Bishkek city demanding Mayor's resignation for allowing the women's rights and LGBT march in the city a few days earlier.

On 24 November 2020, residents of Ak-Ulak village near Min-Kush township in Naryn region protested against the local coal mine, demanding compensation for environmental damages and relocation to safety due to the negative impact of the coal mining operation.

On 23 November 2020, residents of Mady village in Kara-Suu district of Osh region protested against the local donkey farm, demanding the authorities shut it down out of public health concerns in the area.

On 27 November 2020, about 100 residents of the Bazar-Korgon district in Jalal-Abad region protested in the village of Gumkhana, demanding the authorities suspend the local Dashman state reserve's status and allow protesters' livestock to graze on its land.

On 3 December 2020, residents of Deires-Sai village near the town of Kyzyl-Jar in Aksy district of Jalal-Abad region gathered on the disputed border land in protest of demarcation activities by the Uzbek border guards demanding the Kyrgyz authorities address their concerns. Protesters complained the Uzbek border guards moved twenty meters deep into the Kyrgyz territory, which villagers refused to accept.

On 30 November 2020, residents of the bordering Deires-Sai village near the town of Kyzyl-Jar in Aksy district of Jalal-Abad region gathered on the disputed border land between Kyrgyzstan and Uzbekistan after demarcation activity by the Uzbek border guards, which villagers opposed to claiming the Uzbek troops intruded into the territory of Kyrgyzstan. Kyrgyz Border Service refuted protesters' claims indicating that Uzbek demarcation work was in line with the accord from 2017, which both countries agreed upon.

On 11 December 2020, supporters of acting prime minister Sadyr Japarov protested in the town of Kyzyl-Kia in Batken region against his presidential election campaign manager, demanding Japarov's campaign headquarters remove him and replace him with their representative. Protesters accused the manager of having ties to the local corruption-prone establishment.

On 16 December 2020, workers of the resort in Bosteri village of Issyk-Kul region protested on the hotel premises demanding wages for several months of work. Protesters called for authorities to intervene in the labor dispute.

On 23 December 2020, residents of Batken township in Batkern region protested at the Mayor's office against illegal transactions of drainage trays involving the chief of the water fund, whom residents accused of breaking the law, demanding their property back. Protesters insisted trays are owned by the public water trust fund.

On 16 December 2020, residents of Ak-Sai village in Batken district of Batken region blocked the Isfara-Vorukh road to protest increasing incidences of cattle theft, which protesters blamed the Tajik side for. They also demanded that authorities recover their stolen livestock from the Tajik territory. Tajik officials promised to resolve the issues at the meeting with the Kyrgyz counterparts.

On 4 January 2021, residents of Ak-Dobo village in Aksy district of Jalal-Abad region gathered near their homes demanding the local authorities release three of their fellow villagers from police custody or else protesters will be blocking nearby motor road if their demands aren't met.

On 30 December 2020, about 3 persons with disabilities protested at the Mayor's Office in the town of Naryn demanding the local administration transfer the state-subsidized housing property ownership to protesters' households or else they will continue protesting at the townhall indefinitely.

On 23 December 2020, about 20 villagers from Emgeckchil township in Naryn region gathered at the Solton-Sary gold mine demanding the company hire locals for work. Previously, Solton-Sary gold mine was shut down following a conflict between local villagers and the Chinese company in August 2019.

On 2 January 2021, hundreds of supporters of three Aksy district residents, who were detained in October 2020, protested outside the State Security Service office in Jalal-Abad city demanding their release from detention. Protesters called the authorities to arrange house arrest for detained fellows.

On 28 December 2020, residents of Khaidarkan township in Batken region gathered outside a local bank to protest against its closure, demanding the bank's management keep it operating for the town population.

On 22 December 2020, residents of Toktogul district protested at the local district court in Toktogul town against a cattle theft suspect's release to house arrest, which protesters disagreed and deamnded that the suspect be detained and prosecuted.

Around 15 December 2020, residents of Deires-Sai village near the town of Kerben in Aksy district of Jalal-Abad region protested against demarcation works by the Uzbek border guards on the disputed borderland, demanding the Uzbek side stop its activity and move back to the mutually agreed-upon line. Protesters called for the Kyrgyz government to protect the country's land from illegal intrusion.

On 7 January 2021, residents of Ak-Talaa district in Naryn region protested at the local administration in the town of Baetov, demanding the authorities investigate the Turuk Mining company's illegal operation at the nearby coal project. Protesters accused the miner of tax evasion.

On 14 January 2021, 7-8 persons with disabilities protested outside the Naryn regional state administration's office in Naryn city demanding the authorities grant property rights for a municipal residential building to protesters. One of the demonstrators attempted to commit an act of self-immolation but was prevented from doing so by policemen on the scene.

Around 14 January 2021, residents of Ptitsefabrika neighbourhood in Jalal-Abad city protested near their homes against foreigners that were allegedly giving out candy and religious memorabilia to the local children. Protesters called the police while attempting to communicate with foreign citizens.

On 21 January 2021, about 50 residents of Kyzyl-Alma village outside the town of Tash-Komur in Jalal-Abad region protested against nearby coal mining project Shyn-Sai demanding the local authorities close it down due to environmental concerns in the area.

On 27 January 2021, residents of Kaindy township in Panfilov district of Chui region gathered outside the local stadium in support of a Mayor after a recent negative public campaign against town official on social media. Protesters argued life has improved in town under incumbent Mayor's leadership.

On 23 January 2021, activists gathered at the Jerooy gold mine in Talas region demanding the company rebuild its unsafe tailings pond out of concerns for a possible spill of toxic chemicals into the local water sources.

On 7 September 2018, an anti-opposition protest was staged outside the OSCE office in Dushanbe. The protest was organized by the authorities and held at the initiative of the Avangard (Vanguard) group affiliated to the Ministry of Internal Affairs. The demonstrators protested against the participation of the leader of the banned Islamic Revival Party (PIV) and other oppositionists in the OSCE conference. They also held placards reading 'Down with terrorism', 'Down with IRP' and 'We demand that the OSCE office in Dushanbe to be closed' and were also chanting similar slogans. During the protest, the mother of a suspect in the 29 July 2018 deadly attack on foreign tourists in Danghara, poured a liquid over her head and threatened that she would set fire to herself.

On 4 February 2021, about 100 residents of Ak-Suu village in Aksy district of Jalal-Abad region protested outside the local council building demanding the resignation of the head of the village administration following complaints of lack of oversight at the nearby mining projects, which protesters blamed for environmental concerns and safety issues in the area.

On 2 February 2021, about 200 residents of the Jerge-Tal village protested on Turdakun Usubaliyev square in Naryn city, demanding the release of a fellow activist from police custody who was detained a day earlier after raising a pasture land dispute issues in public. Protesters also called for the authorities to resolve the local dispute involving pastures in the district.

On 1 February 2021, residents of Chon-Talaa village in Batken district protested at the regional state administration's office in Batken township, demanding the authorities build a new school for their children in the village after the old facility was destroyed by fire.

On 5 February 2021, about 250 residents of Batken region protested outside the regional state administration in Batken township, demanding the country's newly-appointed Prime-Minister retract and clarify his previous statement regarding part of the bordering Tort-Kul water reservoir that was transferred to Tajikistan.

On 2 February 2021, 20-30 small retail business owners protested outside the city administration in Jalal-Abad city against the planned relocation of the central bazaar to another area demanding the market remain at the same location.

Around 5 February 2021, car drivers gathered at the city police towing park in Jalal-Abad city demanding to stop police discrimination of car owners based on social status, which protesters complained was taking a toll on their income. Protesters alleged police were engaged in targeting lower-class residents with automobiles resulting in parking tickets and towing for illegal parking.

Around 11 February 2021, residents of the Kadji-Say village in the Ton district of Issyk-Kul region protested against the town administration's illegally elected head, demanding a fair and transparent voting process.

On 11 February 2021, residents of the Kosh-Bolot village near the town of Tengi in Ala-Buka district of Jalal-Abad region protested against delimitation work by the Uzbek border guards demanding the Uzbek side vacate the disputed land.

On 11 February 2021, about 50 residents of Ak-Sai village gathered at the local administration in Batken district of Batken region demanding the Kyrgyz government clarify its position regarding disputed borderland near Kapchygai locality where Tajik border guards established permanent presence and restricted access to the Kyrgyz villagers.

On 10 February 2021, residents of the bordering Aksai village gathered at the regional state administration's office in Batken township, demanding the Kyrgyz government remove Tajik border guards and villagers from the disputed pastures until both countries complete the demarcation process.

On 5 February 2021, relatives and supporters of a suspect in a homicide case protested outside the Jumgal district court in Chaek township, demanding his release from police custody and acquitation of criminal charges. Protesters argued it was a domestic violence incident during which a female victim's husband committed a murder, but not a detained suspect.

On 18 February 2021, local youth activists and Muslim religious leaders marched through the village of Ak-Olon in Ton district of Issyk-Kul region against sale and consumption of alcohol beverages in the area.

On 15 February 2021, residents of Razan-Sai, Kurpo-Sai and Kyzyl-Beyit townships gathered in the village of Razan-Sai of Aksy district in Jalal-Abad region in protest of the planned gold mining near the local pastures, demanding the authorities cancel the project out of concerns for water pollution as a result and restricted access to the pastureland.

On 19 February 2021, about 50 residents of Kayragach village in Leilek district of Batken region gathered in protest of the Tajik villagers' gathering on the disputed border following an attempt by a Kyrgyz villager to launch construction on location.

On 14 February 2021, residents of the Kurban-Kara village in Kara-Suu district of Osh region protested near their homes demanding the local authorities fix roads, build medical clinic and resolve land distribution calls for the village community.

On 19 February 2021, hundreds of supporters of a detained former Customs official Raimbek Matraimov marched through the town of Kara-Suu in Osh region demanding his release from custody after Matraimov's arrest day earlier.

On 14 February 2021, residents of the village of Ak-Sai in Batkent district of Batken region gathered in town calling for the country's new president to appoint a competent person to manage and resolve disputes between Tajiks and Kyrgyz. Locals also urged the authorities to provide social benefits and subsidies to the Kyrgyz population on the border.

Around 23 February 2021, residents of Kichi-Jargylchak village gathered near the disputed 1.7 hectares of private land in Jeti-Oguz district of Issyk-Kul region demanding the authorities return illegally privatized land back to the village or else protesters will unilaterally take posession of the land.

On 22 February 2021, residents of Batken region gathered at the regional state administration in Batken township demanding the Kyrgyz government assist them with returning their family members from Tajikistan where relarives of protesters were detained following illegal broder crossing.

On 22 February 2021, residents of Toktogul district protested near the town of Toktogul in Jalal-Abad region caling for the country's new president to intervene in alleged homicide case investigation and stop injustice toward a detained suspect who protesters argued was innocent.

Around 26 February 2021, residents of the Jekendi village in Chon Alay district of Osh region gathered near their homes calling for the Kyrgyz government to resolve the demarcation issues with the bordering Tajikistan which protesters argued would eliminate potential conflicts with the Tajik border guards.

On 4 March 2021, two journalists of Radio's Liberty Tajik Service were attacked by a security personnel of a gas station in Dushanbe, while they were making a video-report on gas price increases. One of the journalists was pushed while another was kicked in the face reportedly for not having a written agreement to film from the management of the gas station. It has been reported that the city police has detained the man who attacked the journalists. The case is currently under investigation.

On 13 November 2018, 40-50 health workers protested outside the Ministry of Health in Bishkek city against proposed optimisation in the country's healthcare which protesters argued would lead to work overload for underpaid medics, and understaffed clinics and hospitals. Protesters called for the top-ranking health officials not to mislead the public regarding average monthly pay for medics which is not 30,000 Som as the health authorities announced previously.

assoc_actor_1: SDPK: Social Democratic Party of Kyrgyzstan
notes: On 16 November 2018, about 150 supporters of former presidential adviser Ikramjan Ilmiyanov (from SPDK) protested in the town of Katran of Leilek district against arrest and detention of former aide demanding his release to house arrest. Protesters appealed to the Kyrgyz president for intervention in the case and fair investigation.
On 26 November 2018, about 100 protesters demonstrated outside the Batken regional administration office in the town of Batken demanding the fair investigation of the murder of a regional prosecutor.

On 5 December 2018, about 20 people gathered in front of the office of the United Nations in Bishkek to raise attention to the situation of minorities including Uyghurs, detained in re-education camps in Xinjiang China.

On 14 September 2018, about 50 residents of the Kyrgyz village of Bakai organized a protest on the borderline near Ak-Sai village against the installation of water pipe on the disputed borderland by the residents of the Tajik neighborhood of Vorukh exclave, Isfara district. About 60 Tajik villagers gathered on the other side of the border and protested against the Kyrgyz counterparts' claim over a contested piece of land.

On 21 May 2019, about 40 residents of Kara-Bak village in Batken district protested during a meeting with local officials and Kyrgyz Border Service officers near local cemetery demanding the Kyrgyz authorities resolve the border dispute with Tajik counterparts over burial ground claim on both sides which protesters argued has been part of the village for many years.

On 6 June 2019, about 15 activists and journalists protested at the Chui regional administration's office in the village of Lebedinovka outside Bishkek city demanding governor of Chui region resign from office and apologize to a female journalist for inappropriate comment regarding her appearance which he made a day earlier.

On 23 January 2019, a group of Tajik residents from Lackan village protested on the borderline near Kara-Bak village in Batken, against the Kyrgyz villagers-built shade structure on the disputed land near the village, demanding its demolition. It sparked a counter-protest by a group of Kyrgyz villagers who have argued it was agreed upon by both sides previously.

On 1 October 2018, more than 10 medics of the Family Medical Center 3 protested outside the clinic in Jalal-Abad city against salary withdrawal for the month of September which protesters argued wasn't consulted with them in advance.
