Protesters announced they will strike if their concerns aren't addressed.

On 4 February 2021, small business owners protested at the central bazaar in Jalal-Abad city against relocation of the market to another location demanding the city administration reject the plan and the city Mayor resign.

On 26 January 2021, residents of the Kara-Bak village in Batken district of Batken region gathered near their homes demanding the local authorities fix the power supply issues in the area which were becoming a safety concern, protesters complained.

On 4 December 2018, 30-40 road construction workers protested at the a company's field office in Kyzyl-Kia town of Batken region, demanding their back pay for more than 6 months of work. Protesters complained the company has been dodging them for weeks which forced protesters to take up the issue with the government.

On 4 March 2019, about 10 neighborhood residents protested at the construction site on Jibek-Jolu street in Bishkek city against the construction of multi-storey apartment building next to their residential homes and demanded to halt the construction activity. Protesters accused the developer company of making fraudulent claims in order to collect approval signatures from the neighborhood folks.

On 17 June 2019, about 30 residents of Jiyde village in Suzak district of Jalal-Abad region protested against the Kyrgyz border post commander whom protesters accused of extorting bribes in exchange OF access to the gravel yard on the dry bed of bordering Karadarya river. Protesters demanded the local authorities intervene in their conflict with the border guards and ensure their way of make a living by collecting gravel.

On 29 June 2018, 20-30 residents of Ak-Suu village near Kerben town in Aksy district of Jalal-Abad region protested against the nearby gold mining project accusing the mining company of engaging in corruption with the local district officials. Protesters demanded transparency for the mining projects and social benefits for the local villages.

On 22 May 2019, 40-50 residents of Papan village in Kara-Suu district protested outside the local administration's office against the newly elected head of the township accusing him of corruption and demanded re-elections.

On 18 June 2019, about 20 residents of Ketmen-Tobo village in Jalal-Abad region protested near their village administration against lack of attention from the district officials to their concerns regarding social grievances and water supply issues which protesters complained have been raised repeatedly by them but without success.

On 10 April 2018, 15-20 residents and rice farmers of Progress village in Uzgen district protested on the disputed land in the village against the local administration's plan to take a portion of their land away. Protesters accused town officials of corruption and land grabbing which violates land lease agreement that stated it was given to the villagers for 99 years.

On 18 April 2019, 30-40 residents of Kara-Jygach village in Suzak district of Jalal-Abad region protested in the village against sublease of agricultural land to them by the local farmer. The protesters argued he may have violated national laws on cropland use. The Protesters demanded the district authorities resolve disputes and lease land without a middleman.

On 22 February 2020, 15-20 residents of Uchar village in Kara-Suu district protested against illegal home building activity in the outskirts of town demanding to stop any construction works due to a risk of seasonal flashflood which protesters argued is likely to happen if illegal construction persists without oversight.

On 6 December 2018, about 20 residents of Yntymak village in Uzgen district of Osh region protested near their homes against the alleged mob-tied local family demanding them to move out of village. Protesters complained they were physically assaulted and threatened with a knife by particular family members on numerous occasions.

Around 29 October 2020, unregistered taxi drivers gathered in Talas city in protest of the newly appointed regional governor's plan to tax cab drivers and waitresses. Protesters called for the central government to dismiss the incumbent governor and appoint a more qualified person to lead the Talas region.

On 15 February 2021, residents of Kosh-Bolot and Kok-Serek villages protested on the disputed border near the town of Tengi in Ala-Buka district of Jalal-Abad region demanding the Uzbek border guards halt demarcation activity at the location and the Kyrgyz government resolve border dispute.

Around 9 November 2020, unemployed coal mine workers and local entrepreneurs gathered at the Kara-Kozu coal deposit near Kyzyl-Kia township in Batken region, demanding the regional and district administrations allow them to operate the mine bypassing the private investor who secured his license after winning an auction which protesters argued was unreasonable due to lack of mining at the location.

On 17 May 2018, 20-30 residents of Turk-Abad village in Suzak district protested against illegal sale of their farmland by the local officials to the non-local buyers demanding criminal investigation and their land plots back.

On 8 April 2019, about 40 residents of Sur and Ormosh villages protested in Sur village in Kadamjai district of Batken region against the local authorities' inaction to prevent infrequent conflicts between the youths of two townships. They also demanded from the district administration to build a separate school for Sur village. The protesters argued conflicts occur due to shared use of one primary school in Ormosh village.

On 30 May 2019, 5-10 villagers of Jiyde township in Suzak district of Jalal-Abad region protested in the village against misconduct and extortion of bribes by the Kyrgyz border guards in exchange for accessing gravel yard on the dry riverbed. The protesters complained they have to pay bribes to the border troops for each trip to collect gravel to make a living. They also accused the border guards of occasionally assaulting some of them.

On 16 February 2021, residents of the village of Tokoy in Kadamjai district of Batken region gathered in town calling for the Kyrgyz authorities to implement CASA-1000 regional power transmission line project away from the village out of public health concerns which villagers complained the government failed to address prior to construction activity.

On 3 June 2019, 30-40 residents of Kojo-Kelen village near the town of Papan in Kara-Suu district of Osh region protested against the imam of the local mosque demanding his resignation due to alleged assault records with his involvement.

On 30 April 2019, 20-30 supporters of imprisoned former lawmaker Sadyr Japarov protested at the Supreme Court hearing in Bishkek city demanding his release from prison and drop all charges against him. Protesters called the case a political witch hunt ahead of parliamentary elections in 2020.

On 19 June 2020, about 10 residents of Tepe-Korgon village in Aravan district of Osh region protested against the local councilman whom protesters allegedly gave $2500 each in exchange for land plots to build homes but they have never been reciprocated. Protesters called for the authorities to intervene and assist them to retrieve their payments.

On 7 February 2018, about 30 supporters of Kyrgyz female lawmaker Aida Salyanova from the Ata-Meken parliamentarian faction protested at the hearing in Bishkek city court against political persecution of opposition politicians in Kyrgyzstan and demanded from the prosecution to drop all charges against the lawmaker.

On 17 May 2019, about 25 residents of Esenbay village protested near the town of Kerkidan in Aravan district of Osh region against lack of drinking water supply and severe water shortage during the summer season demanding the local authorities fix the issue.

On 18 September 2018, dozens of residents of Kok-Serek village, which is near the town of Tengi in Ala-Buka district, demonstrated in the town hall of Tengi town during the meeting of the Kazakhmys company, demanding an investigation into the death of a local villager who worked at the nearby gold mine Bozumchak managed by the company. The scuffle broke out between one demonstrator and the company staffer.

On 10 December 2018, 5-10 homeowners protested near the hippodrome in Bishkek city against the city administration's plan to expropriate their land and homes due to conflicting property ownership claims. Protesters insisted they have legitimate documents to prove their homeownership rights.

On 5 November 2018, about 20 residents of Zhoon-Kungoi village in Suzak district of Jalal-Abad region protested in the village against lack of attention from the local authorities to their issues regarding access to drinking water. Protesters complained local officials have promised them to fix the problem but it was never delivered.

On 24 December 2018, 50-100 residents of Kara-Suu district in Osh region protested outside their local administration in the village of Nariman demanding the immediate resignation of the head of the township due to a court verdict which concluded that the town official's education certificate was forged. Protesters accused town official of corruption after alleged non-transparent deals with public land plots which were designated for the district residents.

On 11 October 2018, about 10 relatives of a deceased victim in a homicide case protested outside the district court in the town of Nookat, Osh, demanding justice and accusing the case judge of corruption as he released the murder suspects from detention.

On 2 February 2021, activists gathered in the town of Suusamyr in Jaiyl district of Chui region to protest the sale of alcohol in the vicinity calling for the local community to join an anti-alcohol consumption campaign and ban alcohol beverages in the area.

On 7 September 2020, about 20 residents of Besh Badam village in Bazar Korgon district of Jalal-Abad region protested against the local authorities approved 1-hectare grazing land transaction involving a businessman demanding the local administration reverse its decision and cancel the land lease deal.

On 1 February 2021, small shop owners protested outside the city administration in Jalal-Abad city against the relocation of the central bazaar to another area demanding the market remain at the same location.

On 20 January 2021, bus drivers protested in Jalal-Abad city against alleged corruption in the regional department of transportation accusing local officials of extorting bribes from drivers for transportation service contracts.

On 2 October 2018, about 3 women organized a protest in the business district of Jalal-Abad city against the number of MPs of the country's parliament and demanded from the Kyrgyz government to scale down the parliament's annual budget and reduce the number of lawmakers from 120 to 60 or less.

On 9 November 2018, more than 10 local activists protested in Jalal-Abad against excessive police action to discourage them from participating in the meeting of opposition groups which has happened on 8 November in Osh city. They were unable to attend a gathering due to police intervention. The protesters also demanded the Kyrgyz government to reduce the number of lawmakers in the country's parliament, create jobs and introduce moratorium to stop price hikes on utilities.

On 7 December 2018, about 10 clients of Amanbank protested outside its local branch in the town of Kurshab in Uzgen district of Osh region demanding their deposits back from the bank. Protesters complained they couldn't withdraw their life savings appealing to the country's president for assistance.

On 10 September 2020, about 10 residents of Alga village in Kadamjai district of Batken region protested near the border with Uzbekistan against the delimitation of the contested 19 hectares of borderland demanding the Kyrgyz government preserve their interests.

On 17 November 2020, residents of Alysh village in Kadamjai district of Batken region protested against the regional power supply project's planned installations through the village, demanding the Kyrgyz government build power supply lines away from town out of public health safety concerns and unresolved compensation dispute.

On 24 July 2019, 10-15 women from Ak-Sai village protested outside the regional administration's office in Batken township against the use of riot police force to suppress demonstration in Ak-Sai village on the same day demanding officials come out and explain reasons behind the deployment of police units against villagers.

On 18 March 2019, about 50 women displaced from Ak-Sai village due to border events protested at the temporary shelter facility in the town of Batken during a meeting with the Kyrgyz government officials, demanding the central authorities to resolve the border disputes with Tajikistan without further delay due to recurring conflicts on the border.

On 5 January 2021, small retail business owners protested at the local bazaar in the town of Batken of Batken region against the upcoming renovation of their market, demanding the bazaar management be removed from office.

On 15 February 2021, residents of the Boz-Adyr village in Batken district of Batken region protested on the disputed land against installation of a utility pole by Uzbek villagers on the disputed land demanding halt to activity until border dispute is resolved by both Kyrgyz and Uzbek governments.

On 11 April 2018, 20-30 residents of Boz-Adyr village in Batken district protested at the local administration against the assault of a fellow Kyrgyz villager by the Uzbek residents of Sokh exclave two days earlier with the demanding the Uzbek authorities to find the responsibles. The protestors also called Kyrgyz authorities to enforce restrictions on the movement of Sokh exclave residents through Boz-Adyr village.

On 27 January 2021, residents of the bordering with Uzbekistan's Sokh district Charbak village in Batken district of Batken region gathered near their homes demanding the Kyrgyz authorities provide water supply to their village or else buy their properties which would let protesters relocate to a more suitable area.

On 11 January 2018, about 30 residents of Chechme village in Kadamjai district of Batken region protested against the border delimitation commission's proposal to transfer local spring over to Sokh exclave of Uzbekistan, which has sparked disapproval among villagers demanding to keep water source in the Kyrgyz territory.

On 31 October 2018, about 20 residents of Tashtak village in Suzak district protested at the local water pump demanding the Kyrgyz-Kuwaiti charity fund As-Salam fix the issue with water supply for more than 20 households, after the fund installed the equipment previously but then cut water supply of a group of residents.

On 24 January 2018, about 20 residents of Jalal-Abad city gathered at the Mayor's Office to protest against the plan to raise water fees, which protesters complained was unaffordable for many residents. Protesters demanded the city administration to address their concerns or they will take the issue up with the central government.

On 1 February 2019, about 10 clients of Amanbank protested outside its branch office in the town of Kurshab in Uzgen district demanding the bank make the refund of their deposits, which protesters complained they couldn't withdraw out of the bank for months as Amanbank denied having their funds.

On 29 November 2018, about 15 clients of Amanbank protested outside the branch of the bank in the town of Kurshab of Uzgen district in Osh region demanding their deposits back from the bank which protesters accused of running a scam. Protesters complained they couldn't withdraw funds for nearly 6 months and called for the Kyrgyz authorities to cancel Amanbank's license.

On 10 October 2018, about 30 produce market traders protested at the local bazaar near the town of Aravan in Osh region against the takeover of the bazaar by the local authorities. Protesters argued the local administration has never invested funds to improve infrastructure around the market.

On 4 October 2018, 10-15 residents of Kelechek neighborhood near the village of Toguz-Bulak in Leilek district protested against lack of social infrastructure such as school and daycare in the village and demanded proper water supply to their households. Protesters accused the local town administration of corruption due to the illegal practice of selling upscale land plots to wealthy buyers.

On 9 August 2019, about 15 residents of Bayish village near Jany-Bazar township in Nookat district of Osh region protested near their homes against the transfer of nearby land to the private companies, which would build asphalt mixing plant, and demanded from the local authorities to reverse their decision. Protesters complained such project would have a negative environmental impact on the village.

On 19 January 2021, residents of the Kalacha village protested near Uch-Korgon township of Kadamjai district in Batken region against alleged corruption involving local officials after illegal land transactions, demanding the authorities investigate and redistribute plots among villagers.

On 16 April 2018, about 30 villagers of Zhapalak town in Osh city suburb protested against nearby waste dump demanding the city authorities to address the negative environmental impact of the dumpsite on the local population. Protesters complained city officials keep promising to resolve the issue, but there was no solution to the problem yet.

On 5 June 2020, about 20 residents of Osor village near Jatan Township in Nookat district of Osh region protested against the local businessman's mining project on the dry bed of the nearby Kyrk-Kol river which protesters complained is altering water flow and threatening their properties along the river.

On 23 November 2020, residents of Pitomnik village near Kara-Suu township of Osh region protested against an unfair land deal with a local businessman in 1991, demanding 25 hectares of agricultural land leased to the villagers.

On 16 December 2019, about 20 residents of Toloikon village near Kara-Suu township in Osh region protested near their homes against road construction project on parts of their land demanding to stop the construction activity and accusing the local councilman of instigating a land dispute in the village.

On 16 October 2018, about 3 female protesters demonstrated in Jalal-Abad city demanding the country's president and government to reduce the number of lawmakers in the parliament from 120 to 35. Protesters called for electoral system reform to boost the proportional representation in the Kyrgyz parliament.

On 21 June 2018, a group of residents on Gorky street in Jalal-Abad city protested against the city firefighting service's poor performance when one of the neighborhood homes caught fire threatening neighboring houses. Protesters complained that the fire trucks arrived on the scene without water to extinguish a source of the fire.

On 21 January 2019, 20-30 traders protested at the local bazaar near the railroad station in Jalal-Abad city against the takeover of their market by the private entrepreneur who launched construction works at the bazaar without notifying protesters in advance. Protesters complained they were told of upcoming rent raises which they opposed and demanded the city administration intervene in the dispute.
Around 25 October 2018 (as reported), a group of local residents protested against the construction of new houses on the main road in the village of Jany-Turmush in Kara-Suu district.

On 7 March 2019, about 15 farmers protested outside the local administration in the town of Taran-Bazar of Suzak district against land auction which protesters argued was conducted without their knowledge. Protesters complained they grow crops on the land that was put up for auction by the local officials.

On 8 January 2019, about 30 residents of Safarovka village in Suzak district protested at the local high school and daycare complex against the authorities' plan to reorganize a complex by separating school and daycare into different entities which have sparked opposition from the local population that believes such move would negatively impact their children.

On 29 June 2020, about 20 residents of Ishkavan village in Kara-Suu district of Osh region protested near their homes against lack of water supply in town demanding the local authorities to address concerns regarding limited access to drinking water.

On 1 February 2021, residents of the Japalak neighborhood in Osh city protested against a nearby metal manufacturing facility demanding the city authorities shut it down due to environmental pollution and public health concerns in the area.

On 10 March 2021, a group of residents and victims of fraud protested outside the city court in Talas city demanding justice and recovery of their funds from the former high ranking law enforcement official and his spouse.

On 9 March 2021, youth activists gathered in the village of Chaek in Jumgal district of Naryn region calling for the local ban on sale of alcoholic beverages in town and demanding the liquor store owners in the area comply with the anti-alcohol ban in the village initiated by activists.

On 9 March 2021, residents of Besh-Terek village in Jumgal district of Naryn region gathered near their homes in protest of lack of drinking water supply calling for the country's authorities to provide access to drinking water which is becoming a public health concern in the area, villagers complained.

On 12 March 2021, youth activists gathered in the village of Baizak in Jumgal district of Naryn region in protest of alcohol beverage availability and sale in town demanding the local liquor stores stop selling alcohol in the village. Activists announced their plan to collect funds and buy off alcohol stock from the stores and destroy it in public.

On 6 March 2021, 20-25 residents of the Kyrgyz village of Sai in Batken district of Batken region gathered on the disputed border land with Uzbekistan's Sokh exclave demanding Uzbek citizens stop working on the land until demarcation is completed. Protest took place after Sokh district residents launched plowing on the crop field to prepare seedbed for the spring season.

On 9 March 2021, residents of the Kuyruchuk village in Jumgal district of Naryn region gathered near the main road in protest of incomplete road construction demanding the authorities finish the job until 15 March or else villagers warned of a road blockade if it remains unfinished.

On 7 March 2021, a scuffle broke out between farm land owners and residents of Kara-Jygach village of Suzak district in Jalal-Abad region that attempted to illegally grab land for housing which was then met with resistance. Policemen were reported on the scene to maintain public order.
On 10 March 2021, about 100 residents of Jumgal district in Naryn region blocked the main motor road in the village of Chaek demanding the authorities fix bad roads in the area.

On 11 March 2021, residents of Nichke-Sai village in Toktogul district of Jalal-Abad region gathered near their homes calling for the country's president to intervene in the suspected homicide case which protesters claimed wasn't homicide but accidental death. Protesters complained police and prosecutor's office ignore witness statements and evidence.

On 17 March 2021, about 50 protesters demonstrated outside the Panfilov district administration in the town of Kaindy in Chui region demanding justice and retrial of cases involving their family members who were imprisoned without sufficient evidence of committing crimes, protesters complained.

On 15 March 2021, about 100 residents of Talas region gathered outside the regional state administration in Talas city demanding the country's government regulate wholesale milk price which protesters complained is inflicting financial losses on the local milk producers due to high cost of cattle feed.

On 18 March 2021, suppliers of milk gathered near the musical school in Talas city demanding that the authorities regulate wholesale milk cost after a recent drop in prices which protesters complained inflicted financial losses on ordinary suppliers.

On 17 March 2021, about 100 residents of Kara-Oy village in Talas district of Talas region gathered in town demanding that the country's president addresses their concerns regarding the negative environmental impact of the nearby Jerooy gold mine on the local community.

On 23 March 2021, residents of the village of Ozgur in Osh region gathered outside the local former sports facility in protest of the village chief's unsanctioned property auction, which resulted in partial demolition of the abandoned facility. Protesters demanded that the village official calls off the auction and allows villagers to rebuild a gym.

On 24 March 2021, residents of Dacha-Rasvet neighborhood in Jalal-Abad city gathered near their homes demanding that the city administration provides access to drinking water, fixes bad roads and upgrades neighborhood's status due to mounting social issues in the residential area.
On 22 March 2021, residents of Auk village in Bazar-Korgon district of Jalal-Abad region gathered near their homes demanding that the local authorities provide land plots for their housing needs.

On 23 March 2021, residents of Ak-Ulak village near the town of Min-Kush in Jumgal district of Naryn region blocked a nearby road in protest of the local coal mining operation and its negative impact on the village demanding that the authorities shut down the coal project or else relocate them to a safer location.

On 29 March 2021, residents of the village of Kayindy gathered at the regional administration's office in Talas city demanding that the authorities fix bad roads in their village which has been a long issue, protesters complained.

On 29 March 2021, coal truck drivers gathered in the town of Balykchy in Issyk-Kul region in protest of violation of the government-regulated minimum pay requirement by the contractor firms. Protesters demanded that the Kyrgyz central authorities intervene to resolve the pay issues.

On 1 April 2021, residents of Savay and Yntymak villages protested outside the local townhall in Savay of Kara-Suu district of Osh region against proposed swap of border land with Uzbekistan which protesters complained would have 50 hectares of their adjacent land transferred to the neighboring country that is not acceptable, villagers argued. Protesters demanded that their interests be included in the border land swap deal.

On 6 April 2021, residents of Bulak-Bashy village in Leilek district of Batken region gathered near their homes demanding that the Kyrgyz authorities and Border Service provide safety and security due to Tajik border guards hostility towards Kyrgyz villagers, protesters complained. Villagers insisted that the Tajik border guards discharge their weapons as a warning to discourage Kyrgyz villagers from growing crops on the disputed border land.

On 6 April 2021, residents of the Kochokor district protested near the town of Kochkor in Naryn region against the Department of Wildlife Conservation initiated tender which saw Kara-Kujur association granted access rights to the hunting grounds in the district. Protesters disagreed with a result demanding that the authorities annul tender outcome and replace the winner with the local wildlife conservation organizations that have more experience, villagers argued.

On 3 April 2021, dozens of coal delivery truck drivers protested near the village of Kochkor in the district of Kochokor in Naryn region against intermediary contractor firms that are in charge of supply operations which resulted in pay dispute between drivers and contractors, protesters complained. Drivers demanded that the Kyrgyz government set up direct pay for the drivers without intermediaries.

On 5 April 2021, about 100 supporters of an activist-blogger Erulan Kokulov gathered outside the police department in the town of Naryn in his support following police questioning on complaint filed by a local businessman. Protesters demanded that police disclose details of complaint and grounds for police probe into blogger's activity.

On 1 March 2021, 10-15 residents of the Internatsionalnoye village in Leilek district of Batken region gathered for a counter-protest on the disputed border between Kyrgyzstan and Tajikistan following Tajik villagers' protest to the Kyrgyz villager's construction work with heavy equipment. Border officials of the both countries were on the scene to de-escalate tensions.

On 10 April 2021, relatives of deceased female victim in homicide case gathered in the village of Kuyruchuk of Jumgal district in Naryn region demanding that the district court carry out justice accusing victim's husband of committing murder and attempting to cover it up.

On 15 April 2021, residents of the village of Murkut in Nookat district of Osh region blocked the access road to the nearby cement plant in protest of the plant management's failure to aid local infrastructure projects and provide construction material demanding that the cement plant stick to the original unofficial deal with the local communities which stipulated its obligations.

On 12 April 2021, about 30 residents of Kairagach village in Kara-Suu district of Osh region protested outside the district administration in the town of Kara-Suu demanding that the authorities build a school in the village, fence off the local river and build sidewalk on the nearby freeway following child molestation incident days earlier.

On 11 April 2021, male protesters gathered at the local polling station in the village of Orok in Alamudun district of Chui region in protest of the 30 percent election quota for female representatives demanding that women candidates crossed out of the ballot list.

On 16 April 2021, residents of Yntymak village near Kara-Suu locality in Ozgon district of Osh region protested against transfer of 50 hectares of border land surrounding Kempir-Abad water reservoir over to Uzbekistan demanding that the country's president and chief border negotiator meet with protesters to discuss their concerns.

On 15 April 2021, residents of Yntymak village in Uzgen district of Osh region gathered near the contested water reservoir on the border with Uzbekistan in protest of the Kyrgyz government's recent plan to transfer water dam to Uzbekistan which protesters disagreed with demanding that the country's top border delimitation official meet with villagers and address their grievances.

On 20 June 2018, in Bishkek a rally was held in front of the Jogorku Kenesh building in support of the arrested Kazakh blogger. At the site of the rally there were relatives of the activist and media representatives. The crowd was watched by security forces. At the protest, his mother asked the President of Kyrgyzstan to intervene in her son's extradition process.

On 18 June 2018, a protest against extradition of Kazakh activist and blogger was held near the building of Kazakhstan's embassy in Bishkek. Blogger's mother, wife and several activists from Kyrgyzstan and Kazakhstan took part in the rally.

On 29 May 2018, about 50 protesters gathered near the Supreme court building in Bishkek, demanding fair trial and justice for the former member of parliament, convicted in 2013 of alleged participation in public unrests. He and his supporters claim that his sentence was politically motivated.
On 24 May 2018, protesters related to labor unions gathered in Bishkek. They were calling for resignation of the chief of national labor union in Kyrgyzstan.

On 21 May 2018, about 50 protesters gathered in front of the embassy of Iran in Dushanbe. They claimed that Iran allegedly supports the banned Islamic Renaissance Party of Tajikistan and demanded stopping it. Police detained several participants and asked them for explanation. According to Eurasianet, the protest was faked by the government.
On 21 May 2018, around 50 protesters gathered in central Bishkek. They were demanding a legal land reform in their respective regions Sokuluk and Alamudun. No detentions were reported.
On 15 May 2018, protesters related to labor unions gathered in Bishkek. They were calling for resignation of the chief of national labor union in Kyrgyzstan.
On 15 May 2018, a mass brawl erupted between villagers of Kyzyl-Unkur over the cutting down of trees. Three people were injured.

On 10 May 2018, traders of the local bazaar in Osh city gathered for a picket protest in Bishkek near the building of the Parliament. On 10 May 2018, protesters gathered in the center of Bishkek in order to support the former administrative deputy. They claimed that his trial was not fair and just, but rather chaotic. Around 500 protesters gathered.

On 7 May 2018, protesters gathered in Bishkek, Kyrgyzstan. They were protesting against administrative moratorium on land transformation in their Alamedin district. According to the attached photo, several dozens of protesters participated.
On 7 May 2018, five policemen beat up a man who was hanging up posters for the 'Immortal Regiment' march, dedicated to Soviet veterans, in Bishkek. He received severe trauma to his head.
On 3 May 2018, a mob attacked a local man in Lipenka, Jeti Oguz, Issyk-Kul, because they suspected him of having molested a young girl. He was hospitalized after. He reportedly had mental problems.
Around 28 December 2018, residents of Min-Bulak held a general gathering to speak out against gold mining on the summer pasture of Solton-Sary.
On 28 December 2018, several citizens gathered in Bishkek to demand tougher punishment for sexual child abuse.

On 24 December 2018, residents of Military Antonovka, Bishkek district gathered in the center, calling deputies to take a legal decision on the land transferring. According to photos provided, dozens of protesters gathered.
On 22 December 2018, members of the Ata Meken party participated in a march celebrating the 60th birthday of the jailed Ata-Meken leader in Bazar-Korgon.

On 20 December 2018, about 200 persons gathered near the Chinese embassy in Bishkek to demand that the authorities of Kyrgyzstan expel within 30 days foreigners illegally residing in Kyrgyzstan. On 18 December 2018, four detainees were beaten and reported torture by law enforcement officers while being transferred from the court to the detention center in Bishkek.

On 17 December 2018, about 10 Chinese Kyrgyz gathered in front of the building of the Ministry of Foreign Affairs of the Republic of Kyrgyzstan in Bishkek as part of the wave of protests against the detention of minorities in Xinjiang China.
On 14 December 2018, an action against the smog started in Bishkek. A rally was organized for the occasion.

On 10 December 2018, a former employee of the Junda plant where violation of workers' right were decried, was beaten by unidentified individuals in Kara-Balta ahead of the visit of parliament's deputies in the city. He was previously threatened and beaten in November.

On 5 December 2018, residents of Bishkek gathered in the center of the capital. They claim that they can be left without houses due to the illegal actions of the prosecutor's office. According to photos provided, dozens of protesters gathered.

On 3 December 2018, about 200 residents of the village of Terek-Say gathered in Terek-Say near Buzuk to demanded the release of the 17 persons who were arrested after fighting with law enforcement. They also demand employment opportunities in the area and protest against the proximity of a plant using cyanide nearby.

Around 23 November 2018, veterans of the Batken gathered in Bishkek to protest against the non-payment of their annual social benefits to the families killed during the Batken event.

Around 21 November 2018, residents of the Muras-Ordo residential complex gathered in Bishkek to demand the opening of a new public transport route.
On 19 November 2018, about 30 doctors gathered in Bishkek to protest against the optimization of family medicine centers proposed by the government.

Around 19 November 2018, the suspect arrested in the case of the murder of a prosecutor was subject to torture in Batken. Around 7 November 2018, three persons were injured as a result of a demonstration that took place in the village of Terek-Say near Buzuk. Two soldiers and one local resident were wounded. This occurred in the context of demonstration against the activities of mining companies in the area.

On 18 October 2018, two residents of the village of Kavok located in southern Tajikistan, were killed by an anti-personnel mine explosion near the village.

On 4 October 2018, a pro-government protest was staged outside the OSCE office in Dushanbe. About 20 young people protested against the presence of the OSCE in Tajikistan accusing it of supporting 'terrorist groups acting against the government of Tajikistan.'

On 3 October 2018, a protest was held by the dismissed teachers of the Republican Olympic Reserve School in Bishkek demanding that they be reinstated at work and that the Director of the school be held accountable for their illegal dismissal.

On 12 September 2018, a short cross-border altercation erupted between residents of a Tajik and a Kyrgyz village when Tajik residents of the Vorukh exclave started the construction of water pipes. The tension was diffused by negotiations between the mayors of the two opposing villages.

On 7 April 2022, residents and councilmen of Kulundu village in Leylek district of Batken region gathered near their homes in protest of lack access to drinking water in the area calling for the country's president to speed up construction of water supply infrastructure in town.

On 12 December 2019, about 100 activists and journalists came out to the main Ala-Too square in Bishkek city wearing respiratory masks in protest of deteriorating air pollution in the country's capital.
On 7 December 2019, about 100 Bishkek city residents including government officials took part in the march against domestic violence at the Yntymak park in Kyrgyzstan.

On 5 December 2019, dozens of residents of Chaek town gathered at the local administration in protest of a lack of access to drinking water in town. Protesters demanded the authorities resolve the issue in a timely manner.

On 3 December 2019, about 20 protesters staged a rally outside the Kyrgyz parliament in Bishkek city demanding reforms of the coutnry's judiciary. Protesters called for the authorities to eradicate corruption in the courts and remove corrupt judges.

On 2 December 2019, a group of conservative activists protested at the Ministry of Culture, Information and Tourism against the feminist art exhibition including images of naked women. On 2 December 2019, an unknown number of villagers in the town of Itagar gathered at the local township administration demanding the police to find the criminals that stole sheep from the village.

On 16 October 2019, the parliament of Tajikistan adopted a law on tougher punishment for torture used by law enforcers against civilians. Earlier it was possible to either pay a fine or serve in prison; now the officials are forbidden also to serve for years after their release from prison. The law was adopted after the recent high rise of cases of torture reported in the country.

On 15 October 2019, about 60 employees of the state building department protested against their leadership due to bribery and corruption in the organization in Kara-Suu. Protesters urged the Kyrgyz authorities investigate corruption in the organization.
On 15 October 2019, about 100 protesters demanded land plots for housing from the city administration in Bishkek. Police detained 5 protesters after illegal attempts to cease land.
On 14 October 2019, according to the Press center of the Supreme Court of RT, the National Alliance of Tajikistan was announced by the Supreme Court an 'extremist' and 'terrorist' organization.

On 14 October 2019, the Supreme court of Tajikistan labelled the National Alliance of Tajikistan (NAT), based in the EU [coded to Dushanbe], as a 'terrorist group'. Representatives of the group denied such accusations and claimed that this is only a way how Dushanbe targets the political opposition.

On 6 October 2019, about 500 local residents protested in Kyzyl-Ozgarysh against the mining exploration activity in the area. Protesters demanded halt to all mining activity and cancel the company's license.

On 1 October 2019, about 100 senior citizen demonstrators organized a Nordic Walk in Bishkek to raise awareness on aging and retirement. Demonstrators put up banners against any form of discrimination toward senior citizens based on their age.

On 30 September 2019, about 60 protesters gathered in Ak-Dobo to protest against an arrest of ex-president's bodyguard. Protesters urged the incumbent president to oversee a fair investigation of the case involving bodyguard.

On 25 September 2019, traders from the local bazaar in Osh gathered near the city hall of the capital Bishkek, demanding solution of various economic challenges related to their trade. Reportedly over 250 traders participated. The authorities promised to find a solution and the crowd dispersed.

On 18 September 2019, delegations of Tajikistan and Kyrgyzstan came to an agreement over the border regime, following the recent deadly clash. Negotiators agreed on demolition of a local controversial observation tower, withdrawal of additional military forces and equipment and eventually organization of joint patrols across the border.

On 17 September 2019, about 15 protesters gathered in Bishkek, outraged by the armed clash in Batken region. Law enforcers called the rally unauthorized and briefly detained about 10 of them and took them away.

The minor was treated in hospital, the policeman denied the statement. [coded according to the version of the civilian]

On 14 September 2019, about 50 protesters gathered in Ak-Sai village, Batken region, expressing their outrage related to the rising tensions on the border with Tajikistan and allegedly illegal construction works conducted by Tajiks in the disputed area.

On 13 September 2019, protesters gathered in Malovodnoye, Kyrgyzstan. They were predominantly parents of local schoolchildren, outraged by economic condition and management of the school fund. The exact number of protesters was not reported.

On 5 September 2019, about 100 residents of Ak-Talaa district gathered to express their discontent with the ongoing construction works in the district and demanded a meeting with the government.

On 3 July 2019, several Tajik residents of Vorukh beat a Kyrgyz man who was selling petrol in the outskirts of Ak-Sai village across the border between Tajikistan and Kyrgyzstan. On 3 July 2019, former President of Kyrgyzstan Atambayev and around 1000 of his supporters held a protest in front of the 'Media Forum' in Bishkek against the government of President Sooronbay Jeenbekov. The protest followed after Jeenbekov had withdrawn immunity status for Atambayev, opening the way for the former President to be persecuted for abuse of power and corruption.

On 1 July 2019, police severely beat and admitted electric shocks to a suspected drug smuggler in Dushanbe until he confessed his crime. Forensic experts later confirmed injuries on his hands and buttocks as a result of electric shocks and beating. [coded according to the civilian's version]

On 27 June 2019, the parliament deprived the immunity status from ex-President Almazbek Atambayev, which allowed law enforcement agencies to prosecute him for corruption. Atambayev believed that this decision was politically motivated by the current President. In response, Atambayev fortified his compound in Koi-Tash and gathered supporters, in order to put up resistance when the authorities try to forcefully arrest him.

On 25 June 2019, supporters of the former President of Kyrgyzstan Atambayev (SDPK) founded a 'National headquarters against lawlessness' in Atambayev's residence in Koi-Tash. Reportedly they decided to stay in the villa to guard him personally. Among others, a group of horsemen arrived and several yurts were erected to accommodate them.
On 25 June 2019, around 50 protesters gathered in Bishkek with pickets, calling authorities to refund money they had previously invested in land plots. No intervention of law enforcers was reported.

On 24 June 2019, members of the trade union federation, various other institutions and students gathered in Bishkek, protesting against adoption of a proposed law on trade unions. Around 100 individuals gathered.

On 24 June 2019, the leader of Hizb ut-Tahrir in Kyrgyzstan was arrested by 'anti-terrorist' police units in Jalal-Abad. The leader is wanted for spreading radicalizing materials and recruiting fighters. Hizb ut-Tahrir is designated a 'terrorist' organization in Kyrgyzstan.

On 23 June 2019, an elderly woman was beaten by a Colonel of the Police Academy in Bishkek. She suffered severe bruises in her face. The police did not respond when she called the police. [coded according to the civilian's version]

On 22 June 2019, young local residents of Bishkek held a public flash mob, theatrical performance and art exhibition. The aim was to raise awareness about violence against women, especially bride kidnapping, early marriages and limited education provided.

On 17 June 2019, members of the trade union federation gathered in Bishkek, protesting against adoption of a proposed law on trade unions. The exact number of protesters was not revealed.
On 13 June 2019, journalists of RFE/RL were attacked by bus driver and conductor in Dushanbe during filming videos for a reportage. The bus driver was later fined.

On 10 June 2019, a rally took place in Dushanbe near a building of the EU and the UN. Reportedly, dozens of protesters gathered, demanding the Tajik lawyer working for these organizations not to be released from prison; claiming that he is a criminal and such foreign organization shall not interfere into Tajik internal affairs.

On 9 June 2019, about 30 residents of Ak-Suu reportedly attacked a couple because of conflicts on using water in summer months. Among the attackers there were reportedly also a member of local administration and his family.

The exact number of protesters was not revealed.

On 5 June 2019, a skirmish among three teenagers resulted in mob violence among two groups of residents of the Orok municipality. The clash was reportedly fought between two ethnic groups, as one of the participants was a Turk and the others Kyrgyz. Several houses were damaged. Law enforcers intervened and 31 individuals were detained, 24 of whom were released after questioning.

On 4 June 2019, residents of Andarak village, Leilek district gathered for a rally and blocked a local road. They were protesting against alleged misuse of forest land in a disputed area nearby. Reportedly, around 60 protesters gathered.

On 3 June 2019, Kyrgyz residents of Iskra settlement, Leylek district gathered and blocked a local road; Tajik civilians were present as well. They demanded to tear down a house, which was reportedly being under construction illegally by Tajiks. Law enforcers came into place with local administration to calm the situation. No detentions reported. Exact number of participants was not revealed.
On 31 May 2019, a resident of Bishkek was detained and reportedly beaten in order to confess to an alleged crime. Medical examination confirmed the physical attack.

On 30 May 2019, about 40 protesters, predominantly single mothers and health workers gathered for a rally in the center of Bishkek, demanding proper housing and social support of single mothers and state employees, especially health workers employed at a local clinic.
On 30 May 2019, a female resident of Bishkek was detained and reportedly beaten in order to confess to an alleged crime. Medical examination confirmed the physical attack.

On 27 May 2019, about 100 supporters of the former bodyguard of the President Atambayev, gathered in the village Bokonbayevo, Ton district, protesting against his imprisonment, allegedly politically motivated. He was sentenced to 5 years over corruption when was serving as the head of the State Security Service.

On 27 May 2019, residents of Ak-Suu District gathered for a rally protesting against alleged illegal construction at local recreation center and other violations of law related to the facility, as well as being concerned by environmental issues. According to the photos provided, around one hundred of protesters gathered.
On 25 May 2019, a resident of Bishkek was detained and reportedly beaten in order to confess to an alleged theft. Medical examination confirmed the physical attack.

On 16 May 2019, about 200 supporters of the former bodyguard of Kyrgyzstan President, gathered in the village Bokonbayevo, Ton district, protesting against his imprisonment, allegedly politically motivated. On 13 May 2019, about 30 residents of Ak-Suu gathered for a rally protesting against alleged illegal construction at local recreation center and other violations of law related to the facility, as well as being concerned by environmental issues.

On 6 May 2019, a civilian was reportedly detained and beaten by law enforcers in Alamedin district of Bishkek city. The information was provided by the National Center for the Prevention of Torture. Reportedly, he was beaten and choked in order to provide confession to an alleged crime.

On 2 May 2019, around 50 protesters gathered in Karakol, protesting against uranium mining in Kyrgyzstan. Activists and organizers believe that the industry puts the environment in danger.

On 30 April 2019, over 20 people gathered with pickets in the center of Bishkek, protesting against the development of the Tash-Bulak uranium deposit in Issyk-Kul and for public hearings on this matter. The event was co-organized by the Green party of Kyrgyzstan.

On 30 April 2019, protesters gathered in Balykchy, protesting against the development of the Tash-Bulak uranium deposit in Issyk-Kul and for public hearings on this matter. Around 500 protesters gathered for the event. The event was a part of wider string of demonstrations against the deposit.

On 29 April 2019, dozens of protesters gathered in Dushanbe near the offices of Faroz company, owned by President's son-in-law, demanding changes in internal economic cut-off of fees and pays. Around 15 protesters were briefly detained.

On 29 April 2019, residents of Alai, Osh region attacked a group of disabled (deaf) people on a false information; thinking that they were pedophiles and wanted to abduct local children. They were beaten and their car was damaged. Later it appeared that the rumor had been spread by children, who had previously harassed the deaf people themselves.

On 26 April 2019, protesters gathered in the center of Bishkek, protesting against development of the Tash-Bulak uranium deposit in Issyk-Kul and for public hearings on this matter. About 300 protesters reported. The event was a part of wider string of demonstrations against the deposit.

On 22 April 2019, violent demonstrations of local Tajik and Kyrgyz villagers occurred in their respective villages in the border area. On 17 April 2019, from 20 to 25 anti-corruption police officers beat a lawyer in Dushanbe in order to make him confess to corruption. The beating resulted in six broken teeth and severe bruises to his face.

notes: On 31 March 2019, two officers detained a drunk man in Dushanbe for urinating in public and beat him in his crotch. He was hospitalized with ruptured testicles and severe damage to his groin, and permanently lost functionality of his sexual organs.

On 28 March 2019, a resident of Bishkek was arrested and beaten by law enforcers, because he accidentally hit their car with a stone. Allegedly, he was later forced to sign a statement that he injured himself. Later he had to be transferred to a hospital.

On 27 March 2019, a rally was held in Bishkek in front of the Russian embassy on the eve of the planned visit of Russian President to Kyrgyzstan. On the posters that were carried by the protesters were inscriptions such as 'Putin is the aggressor', 'Occupant', 'Killer' and also 'We don't need a military base'. Kyrgyz police considered these announcements as a call for ethnic hatred and detained two of the protesters.

On 26 March 2019, a protest was held in front of the mayor's office in Balykchy by the residents of the city. The protest was organized against the development of the Tash-Bulak alluvial uranium deposit in the Ton district. The event was a part of wider string of demonstrations against the deposit.

On 25 March 2019, a protest was held in front of the mayor's office in Bishkek by forty mothers of children infected with HIV from 2004 to 2007 in the hospitals of Kara-Suu, Nookat and Ozgonsky districts.
On 23 March 2019, a Tajik national was killed by an anti-personnel mine when picking herbs in a hilly area on the border with Afghanistan in the Shohin District of the southern Khatlon Region.
On 20 March 2019, around 20 protesters gathered in the center of Bishkek to support the ex-chairman of the NSC of Kyrgyzstan, demanding his immediate release.

On 20 March 2019, around 15 residents of Kara-Suu district gathered for a rally, calling for resignation for the head of local administration. Reportedly, police officers asked the protesters to leave, but without any further intervention.
On 18 March 2019, a protest was held near mayor's office in Bishkek by the residents of the city. The protesters expressed disappointment with conditions in minibuses and demanded their improvement.
On 17 March 2019, a requiem rally for those killed in the Aksy events took place on Ala-Too Square in Bishkek.

On 14 March 2019, protesters gathered in Bakai-Ata district, Talas oblast, [coded to Bakai-Ata] calling for closing development of the Shyralzhyn mining field. The protests are linked to a disappearance of an employer of the field, who went missing in January. The exact numbers of protesters was not revealed, but according to photos there were dozens of individuals.

On 13 March 2019, a rally of the public association 'Kyrk Choro' was held near the city hall building in Bishkek. The participants of the rally protested for banning LGBT 'propaganda' and against the march organized by feminist groups on March 8.

According to 24kg, firearms also have been deployed. Kyrgyzstan's border guards intervened and stopped the conflict. On 11 March 2019, five women were protesting in Naryn against the decision of local government to demolish their trade objects and even announced their preparedness to start a hunger strike. Later they managed to set an appointment with the local governor and decided to disperse.

On 7 March 2019, traffic police detained a man suspected of car theft in Dushanbe, took him to the police station and beat and kicked him to his head and stomach. On 26 February 2019, law enforcers of the Oktyabrsky district, Bishkek, reportedly detained and severely beaten a local resident. He ended up with broken spine and other injuries. Later he was hospitalized.

On 23 February 2019, police physically intimidated a lawyer at a police station in Bishkek who was defending the case of a Christian man who had been attacked in Tamchy in October 2018. Police officers reportedly insulted her and the Christian faith, pushed her against the wall and demanded her to drop the case. She fell ill with heart problems as a result, but police prevented her from taking an ambulance to the hospital. After the lawyer was released she was hospitalized for two days. Authorities later denied that the incident had taken place. [coded according to civilian's version]

On 22 February 2019, around 50 protesters gathered in Bishkek. They were predominantly parents of students of a local Turkish school, protesting against the dismissal of the director of the school. He was accused of pedophilia, but the parents claim that all accusations are false and expressed their support to the director.

On 6 February 2019, around 100 protesters gathered in Bishkek. They were protesting against the chairman of the Federation of Trade unions, demanding his dismissal, reportedly due to his insufficient working performance. Later they declared that they are ready to hold another rally again.

On 18 January 2019, in Bishkek, a large group of Kyrgyz citizens took part in a rally in honor of the 76th anniversary of the breakthrough of the blockade of Leningrad. The participants of the rally laid flowers and honored the memory of those who died during the blockade by a minute of silence.

On 18 January 2019, a handful of journalists of the newspaper 'Super-Info' and Next TV channel held a protest in front of the building of Bishkek State Police Department. On 17 January 2019, about 70 residents of Tash-Aryk village in Kyrgyzstan held a protest near the museum complex 'Manas Ordo', demanding the dismissal of the director of the museum. On 15 January 2019, about 30 market traders held a protest at Orto-Sai market in Bishkek, demanding to stop increasing the rent of the retail outlets.

On 11 January 2019, in Ananyevo, Issyk Kul, Kyrgyzstan, a Christian cemetery was desecrated by unknown vandals, who damaged 89 gravestones. Christian cemeteries have been targeted more often in the past.

Tajik and Kyrgyz border guards appeared on the scene to maintain security in the area.

On 16 April 2022, farmers gathered near their crop fields in the village of Zhany-Jer in Leylek district of Batken region in protest of lack of irrigation water supply calling for the country's president to assist them with resolving the issues due to failure to act by the local authorities.

On 21 April 2022, about 20 protesters demonstrated outside the regional prosecutor's office in Talas township against alleged corruption in the office demanding that the Kyrgyz government lustrate local prosecutors and dismiss chief prosecutor.

On 9 June 2018, in Bishkek, around 250 people protested against bride kidnapping in the country. The protest was organized and conducted by students. Earlier a man kidnapped a woman with an intention to marry her and stabbed her to death in a police station in northern Chuy Region.

On 25 November 2019, 5-10 residents of Karakol city gathered at the local stadium in protest against the corruption in Kyrgyzstan. Protesters demanded eradication of corruption in the country.

On 19 February 2024, about 50 drivers of the Russia-based Yandex taxi ride hailing service protested on Osh city against low rates for ride fare.

On 14 July 2020, an automobile belonging to the Tajik representative of the Vorukh township administration of Sughd region was attacked by a group of Kyrgyz nationals on Isfara-Vorukh motor road while driving through the Kyrgyz territory [Batken coded with GP2]. Kyrgyz villagers threw rocks at the vehicle destroying its rear window.

As reported on 11 October, during late September and early October law enforcers organized raids across Tajikistan [coded to Dushanbe], forcibly abducting young men from streets or from their homes and drafting them to the army as conscripts. Their relatives claim that even those who have legal exemption are taken; there were reports of violence used against civilians that defended the young people. The raids were confirmed also by a journalist, who was by chance taken by law enforcers as well, but later released.

On 1 December 2018, a protest was organized in Khorugh following an accident involving a drunk police officer and a minivan which resulted in the death of two persons. They are asking for the punishment of the law enforcement officer.

On 28 October 1622 prisoners were released from Dushanbe prison according to the new amnesty law. Due to the fact that a number of the prisoners were allegedly sentenced to imprisonments because they were affiliated with prominent opposition members, this event may be viewed as strategically important.

notes: On 20 May 2020, at least 60 Chinese workers demonstrated at the mining company they worked in Zarnisor, Sughd demanding their unpaid salaries and return to their homes in China over concerns with regard to the spread of coronavirus and alleged absence of adequate medical facilities as well as that Tajik workers are favored over them. Tajikistan's Ministry of Interior claimed that the demonstrators entered the head office with stick and batons upon which OMON Police intervened by shooting into the air and clashing with the workers with bludgeons. Reportedly 45 workers were injured.

On 29 May 2020, three unidentified men attacked and beat the renown Tajik journalist in Bokhtar during his report coverage from the place of the natural disaster, where people still live in tents. This is the second attack on the same journalist in May. According to the report one the men named himself to be the head of the Jamoat (local administration).

When agents were in the process of moving arrested villagers to the border post nearby, villagers freed 3 detainees using physical force at the GKNB station in town. So far there hasn't been additional reporting with detailed description of how rioters retrieved detainees out of either GKNB vehicle or GKNB station.

On 1 August 2020, about 20 victims of a housing scheme gathered on Rudaki street in Dushanbe city to demand help from the government. The protesters claimed that they have been paying to a residential construction project, but the court canceled their contracts and left them without apartments.

Around 12 August 2020, an unknown number of protesters demonstrated in the town of Vanj of vanj district in Gorno-Badakhshan Autonomous Region against the local authorities forcing beard shaving campaign in the district.

On 29 August 2020, dozens of workers of the central market in the southern district of Shaartuz (Khatlon) protested against the distribution of space in the new market, which caught fire three months ago and was reopened after renovations. The protestors said the distribution of their place in the market was incorrect and demanded the full restoration of their place in the new market.

Property destruction: On 4 November 2020, policemen opened gunfire in Khorugh city of Gorno-Badakhshan Autonomous Region of Tajikistan and damaged civilians' cars. The reasons for the gunfire were unknown. According to the media, no casualties occurred.

Around 1 March 2024 (week of), relatives and family members of a missing 19-year-old woman gathered in the town of Kant in Ysyk-Ata district of Chui region in protest of a failure by police to investigate the case of a missing person calling for the authorities to provide assistance family and relatives of a missing female with search efforts. Police launched probe into the missing person case.

On 2 March 2024, dozens of victims of financial fraud by the ChargeFast firm gathered outside the office of the State Committee on National Security in Kara-Kul township of Kara-Kol district in Jalal-Abad region in protest of fraudulent activity calling for the authorities to recover their funds from fraud scheme masterminds.

On 2 March 2024, residents of Muras-Ordo village near Kok-Jar township in Alamudun district of Chui region gathered by the local lake, where 2 children drowned hours earlier by accident, in protest of transportation of deceased kids' bodies to the morgue for autopsy and the authorities' failure to install fence around the lake prior to the fatal accident, demanding that the authorities secure the area around the lake and allow parents of deceased children claim their bodies.

On 9 March 2024, supporters of a detained activist protested in the town of Doboluu near the village of Alysh in Naryn region against his arrest calling for the country's president to release him from custody.

On 9 March 2024, supporters of detained politician Azimbek Beknazarov protested in the village of Kara-Suu near the town of Kerben in Aksy district of Jalal-Abad region against his persecution, demanding his release from custody and stop persecution of political opponents.

On 11 March 2024, councilmen of the Ak-Dobo village council gathered in the village near the town of Bakai-Ata in Talas region in protest of unsatisfactory administrative-territorial reform in the Bakay-Ata district, which was implemented without public opinion, calling for the country's president to ensure that the local authorities include public voice in the process of reform implementation.

On the night of 18 November, the prosecutor of the Batken region was killed from a blow in the head with a blunt object. The body was found in the outskirt of Batken near the Tort-Kul reservoir, and a suspect was identified.

On 6 October 2020, about 300 supporters of the pro-government Birimdik party demonstrated in solidarity with the country's president and government on the main square in Osh city, condemning the anti-election protest movement in Bishkek city calling it illegal and a coup. A journalist of the RFE/RL was beaten by a group of demonstrators while covering the demonstration. The anti-government demonstrators held a simultanous rally by the same square and severely beat up the chairman of the Osh city council, who also represents the pro-government Mekenim Kyrgyzstan party, as he was leaving the administrative office near the square.

On 6 October 2020, about 500 people gathered on the main square in the town of Uzgen of Osh region demanding the resignation of the town Mayor following anti-government demonstrations across the country. Demonstrators tried to enter the regional state administration building, breaking the windows as district officials came out to calm the situation.

On 27 March 2024, policemen physically assaulted a male resident and his son during detention when victim and his son resisted arrest at the local market in Kochkor-Ata township near the village of Kyzyl-Alma in Nooken district of Jalal-Abad region resulting in minor sustaining injuries requiring hospitalization. Victims' family members filed complaint with the prosecutor's office against police action.

On 28 March 2024, male police officer physically assaulted a local resident in the village of Novopokrovka near the town of Kant in Ysyk-Ata district of Chui region resulting in victim sustaining critical injuries following which the victim was hospitalized. Policeman was detained and placed in custody after the incident.

On 2 April 2024, about 100 cargo truck drivers protested outside the Department of Transportation and Roads in Osh city demanding a meeting with a Minister to address their concerns regarding new rules on the Uzbekistan's border, which are creating legal barriers for truck drivers to deliver goods from China.

On 15 April 2024, five male individuals physically assaulted an off-duty police investigator in the town of Kant of Ysyk-Ata district in Chui region resulting in officer's hospitalization with severe injuries. On 15 April 2024, about 40 residents of Otuz-Adyr village gathered outside the district prosecutor's office in Kara-Suu township of Osh region in protest of unsatisfactory police response to rape incident involving a 5-year-old girl demanding that the authorities transfer victim to the medical hospital for observation.

On 16 April 2024, residents of Otuz-Adyr village near the town of Ozgur in Kara-Suu district of Osh region rallied after a kidnapping and potential sexual assault of a girl, demanding council raise the issue of public safety and that councilmen ban sale of alcohol beverages in the area.

On 16 April 2024, supporters of a member of parliament gathered outside the governor's office in Talas city in protest of his election results dispute in the court calling for the country's leadership and the Supreme Court to ensure that lawmaker's election results in Talas region will not be overturned.

On 19 April 2024, residents of Kara-Kul township in Kara-Kol district of Jalal-Abad region gathered near their homes in protest of local police failure to investigate sexual abuse of a female minor by adult male calling for the country's government to toughen laws against child molesters.

On 21 April 2024, residents of Chym-Korgon village in Kemin district of Chui region gathered near their homes in protest of a local underage male teenager's inappropriate behavior toward younger school students demanding that accused teenager's family relocated away from the village.

On 19 April 2024, a group of residents of Kok-Jar village in Alamudun district of Chui region gathered near their homes in protest of the authorities' plan to incorporate the village into the city of Bishkek.

On 9 March 2024, about 30 supporters of detained activist protested in the village of Kok-Tokoy near the town of Aral in Talas region demanding his release from detention and transparent court hearings in another activist's case.

On 4 December 2023, small retail shop owners protested at the Bulak market in Tokmok city of Chui region against the new tax regime requiring mandatory usage of cash register machines by small businesses demanding that the authorities allow retailers to operate under business patent license.

On 31 August 2023, a male police officer was engaged in indiscriminate shooting while intoxicated using his service pistol in the town of Tokmok in Chui region resulting in private property damage in the residential area. Police detained suspected policeman after the incident.

On 16 August 2023, residents of Aral village in Chui region gathered by Hawaii wellness center outside the city of Tokmok in protest of the planned transfer of local pasture land ownership to the wellness center demanding that the authorities cancel the land transaction and return grazing land back to the villagers.

On 16 June 2023, land farm owners gathered by the BCHK water canal (Great Chui Canal) in Iskra village outside Tokmok township in Chui region in protest of evening and night time restrictions on irrigation water supply in the area demanding that the authorities lift ban or else farmers warned that they will be forced to distribute water from BCHK at will.

On 26 December 2022, workers of the municipal heat supply company protested on the premises in Tokmok city of Chui region against removal of their former director demanding his reappointment back in position.

On 24 October 2022, about 100 supporters of the Kyrgyz government gathered in the town of Tokmok in Chui region to express their solidarity with the country's leadership calling for unity in the nation.

On 14 October 2022, residents of Maksat village in Leylek district of Batken region gathered near their homes in protest of a lack of safety on the border with Tajikistan calling for the country's president to finalize delimitation and demarcation on the border.

On 4 October 2022, residents of Arka-2 village of the Leylek district in Batken region gathered outside their homes in protest of the Kyrgyz government's inadequate response to the border clashes demanding that the country's authorities resolve the border dispute or provide arms to the local population.

On 23 June 2021, a male resident of Iskra village near Tokmok city in Chui region was assaulted by the villagers from a neighboring town following a water dispute resulting in hospitalization of the victim. On 17 May 2021, residents of Jany-Jer village near the town of Arka in Leylek district of Batken region protested outside their homes against smuggling activity through their territory on the disputed Kyrgyz-Tajik border.

On 4 May 2021, residents of Jashtyk village in Leylek district of Batken region blocked the local road to fuel tanker trucks which were allegedly smuggling petroleum supplies to the neighboring Tajikistan, demanding that truckers turn and drive back due to public boycott of trade with the Tajik businesses following a deadly border conflict days earlier.

At least 6 reps of the special forces were taken hostage after the attack and were held throughout the night. 80 people were injured during the clash, including 15 police officers. Atambayev's immunity had previously been withheld and he had refused to comply with two subpoenas.
